blocks:
Sūrat al-Nūr

Medinan, and it is 64 verses

Sūrat al-Nūr

Medinan, without dispute The number of its verses is sixty-four verses according to the Iraqi and Shami counts, and two verses according to the Hijazi count. Its points of difference are two verses: بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ and يَذْهَبُ بِالْأَبْصارِ, both of which are Iraqi. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) سُورَةٌ أَنْزَلْناها وَفَرَضْناها ** (1) A Sura which We have sent down and which We have enjoined And We enjoined what is in it of rulings (الأحكام). And it was recited with tashdid [i.e., وَفَرَّضْنَاهَا]. وَأَنْزَلْنا فِيها آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ (And We sent down in it manifest signs), clear in their indication, لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (1) that you may take admonition. So that you may guard against forbidden things (المحارم). (٢) الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي فَاجْلِدُوا كُلَّ واحِدٍ مِنْهُما مِائَةَ جَلْدَةٍ ** (2) As for the fornicatress and the fornicator, strike each of them a hundred lashes, In al-Qummi, it is stated that this verse abrogates His saying: وَاللَّاتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ [The women among you who commit indecency] and the rest of the verse. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in a hadith: Sūrat al-Nūr was revealed after Sūrat al-Nisa'. The confirmation of this is that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed in Sūrat al-Nisa': وَاللَّاتِي يَأْتِينَ الْفاحِشَةَ مِنْ نِسائِكُمْ ** up to His saying: لَهُنَّ سَبِيلاً ** [a way out for them]. The way (السبيل) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned is سُورَةٌ أَنْزَلْناها ** [A Sura which We have sent down] up to His saying: مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. [of the believers]. And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: If a free man (الحرّ) and a free woman (الحرّة) commit fornication (زنا), each of them is to be lashed one hundred lashes. As for the married man (المحصن) and the married woman (المحصنة), stoning (الرجم) is upon them. And narrated from him عليه السلام: Stoning (الرجم) is in the Quran, His Almighty saying: "The old man (الشيخ) and the old woman (الشيخة)—if they commit fornication (زنا)—stone them absolutely, for they have satisfied their lust." In al-Qummi: The verse of stoning (آية الرجم) was revealed concerning the old man (الشيخ) and the old woman (الشيخة): "If they commit fornication (زنا), stone them absolutely, for they have satisfied their lust, as a deterrent punishment (نكالاً) from Allah, and Allah is All-knowing, All-wise (عليم حَكيمٌ)." And in both (al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib), and in one narration: Concerning the old man (الشيخ) and the old woman (الشيخة), the punishment is lashing (الجلد) then stoning (الرجم). And in another narration: Concerning the married man (المحصن) and the married woman (المحصنة), it is likewise. And concerning the unmarried man (البكر) and the unmarried woman (البكرة), it is one hundred lashes and exile (نفي) for one year outside their city (مصرهما). These are the two who have concluded the marriage contract (أملكا) but have not consummated it (لم يدخل بها). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him عليه السلام: He was asked about the married man (المحصن), and He said: "He who commits fornication (يزني) while having what suffices him." And in both (al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib), narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Whoever has a private part (فرج) to which he goes in the morning and returns in the evening, he is considered married (محصن). And narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: He was asked about the slave girl (الجارية): Does she count as married (تحصن)? He said: "Yes, it is only based on the aspect of sufficiency (الاستغناء)." It was asked: "Temporary marriage (المتعة)?" He said: "No, that is only based on the permanent thing (الشّيء الدائم)." And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Neither the man nor the woman is to be stoned (يرجم) until four witnesses testify against them regarding the sexual intercourse (الجماع), the penetration (الإيلاج), and the insertion (الإدخال) like the needle (الميل) in the kohl container (المكحلة). I say: The reason (العلّة) for requiring four witnesses will come later, if Allah wills. And narrated from al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah: That Umar was brought five individuals who had been apprehended for fornication (الزّنا). He ordered that the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) be carried out on each of them. The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام was present and said: "O Umar, this is not their ruling (حكمهم)." He said: "Then you carry out the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) upon them." So he brought forward one of them and struck his neck (beheaded him). He brought forward the second and stoned him (رجمه). He brought forward the third and struck him with the prescribed punishment (الحد). He brought forward the fourth and struck him with half the prescribed punishment (نصف الحدّ). He brought forward the fifth and administered a discretionary punishment (عزّره). Umar was perplexed, and the people were astonished by his action. Umar said to him: "O Abu al-Hasan, five individuals in one case, and you carried out five prescribed punishments upon them, none of which resembles the other!" The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: "As for the first, he was a protected non-Muslim (ذمّياً) who violated his covenant (ذمّته), and his prescribed punishment (حدّ) was nothing but the sword (السيف). As for the second, he was a married man (محصن), and his prescribed punishment (حدّه) was stoning (الرّجم). As for the third, he was unmarried (غير محصن), and his prescribed punishment (حدّه) was lashing (الجلد). As for the fourth, he was a slave (عبد), so We struck him with half the prescribed punishment (نصف الحدّ). As for the fifth, he was insane (مجنون), overcome by his intellect (مغلوب على عقله)." And al-Qummi is similar, except that he said: "Six individuals." He said: "And he released the sixth." Then he said: "As for the fifth, that act was committed by him due to a misconception (بالشبهة), so We administered a discretionary punishment (عزرناه) and disciplined him (ادّبناه). As for the sixth, he was insane (مجنون), overcome by his intellect (مغلوب على عقله), and accountability (التكليف) is lifted from him." And in both (al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib), narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, He said: "The man is struck with the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) standing, and the woman sitting. Every limb is struck, but the head and the private parts (المذاكير) are left alone." And narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: He was asked about the fornicator (الزاني): How is he lashed? He said: "The severest lashing." It was asked: "Over the clothes?" He said: "No, rather he is stripped naked (يجرّد)." I say: The rest of the rulings (الأحكام) should be sought in al-Wafi. وَلا تَأْخُذْكُمْ بِهِما رَأْفَةٌ ** (2) and let no pity for them detain you Mercy (رحمة). And it was recited with the fathah on the hamzah [i.e., رَأْفَةٌ]. فِي دِينِ اللهِ (in the religion of Allah), meaning: in obedience to Him and in establishing His prescribed punishment (حدّه), so that you do not suspend it or be lenient concerning it. And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, He said regarding the establishment of the prescribed punishments (الحدود): إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ ** (2) if you believe in Allah and the Last Day. For faith (الإِيمان) necessitates seriousness in obedience to Allah and diligence in establishing His rulings (أحكامه). وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذابَهُما طائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (2) And let a group of the believers witness their punishment. In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, He said: وَلْيَشْهَدْ عَذابَهُما ** [And let a group witness their punishment] means their lashing (ضربهما). طائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** [a group of the believers] means people are gathered for them when they are lashed. And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, He said: The group (الطائفة) is one [person]. And in al-Ghawali, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, He said: The attending group (الطائفة الحاضرة) is one [person]. And in al-Jawami', narrated from him عليه السلام: The minimum of it is one man.

(3) الزَّانِي لا يَنْكِحُ إِلَّا زانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَالزَّانِيَةُ لا يَنْكِحُها إِلَّا زانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ وَحُرِّمَ ذلِكَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (3) The fornicator shall marry none but a fornicatress or an idolatress, and the fornicatress shall be married by none but a fornicator or an idolater. This is forbidden to the faithful.

Al-Qummi: It is a refutation against those who deem permissible enjoying (التمتع) the fornicatresses (الزواني) and marrying them, and they are those who are notorious (المشهورات) and well-known (المعروفات) in the world; a man is unable to make them chaste (تحصينهن). He said: And this verse was revealed concerning the women of Mecca who were openly committing fornication (الزنا): Sarah, Khuthaymah, and Al-Rabab, who were singing in satire (هجاء) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So Allah forbade marrying them, and the ruling applied after them to women who were their likes (أمثالهن).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq عليه السلام: That He was asked about this verse, and He said: They are women notorious for fornication (الزنا) and men notorious for fornication (الزنا); they became notorious for it and were known for it. And people today are in that same position (المنزلة). So whoever has the prescribed punishment (حدّ الزنا) carried out upon him, or becomes notorious for fornication (الزنا), it is not proper (لم ينبغ) for anyone to marry him/her until repentance (التوبة) is known from him/her.

And from Him عليه السلام: That is only concerning public (الجهر) acts. Then He said: If a person commits fornication (زنى) and then repents (تاب), he may marry wherever he wishes.

And from Al-Baqir عليه السلام: They are men and women who, during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, were notorious for fornication (الزنا). So Allah forbade [marrying] those men and women. And people today are in that same position (المنزلة): whoever becomes notorious for any part of that, or has the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) carried out upon him, then do not marry him/her until his/her repentance (توبته) is known.

And from Him عليه السلام, in a narration (حديث): That it was revealed in Medina (المدينة). He said: So Allah did not name the fornicator a believer (مؤمناً) nor the fornicatress a believer (مؤمنة). He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The fornicator does not commit fornication (يزني) while he is a believer (مؤمن), nor does the thief steal while he is a believer (مؤمن)." For when he does that, faith (الايمان) is stripped from him like the stripping off of a shirt (القميص).

(٤) وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَناتِ (4) And those who accuse chaste women accuse them of fornication (الزّنا) ثُمَّ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَداءَ فَاجْلِدُوهُمْ ثَمانِينَ جَلْدَةً (4) then do not bring four witnesses, flog them with eighty lashes. There is no difference between the male and the female in either party.

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), concerning a man who accuses another man of fornication (الزنا). He said: "He is flogged according to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), concerning a woman who accuses a man. He said: "She is flogged eighty lashes. But if one of them was a boy (غلاماً), a girl (جارية), or insane (مجنوناً), the hadd (الحدّ) is not applied, as the narrations from them (peace be upon them) have stated."

And in both (al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "If a slave (العبد) accuses a free person (الحرّ), he is flogged eighty lashes." He said: "And this is among the rights of the people (حقوق الناس)."

And from him (peace be upon him): "If a man who has accused a Muslim slave (عبداً مسلماً) of fornication (الزنا), and nothing but good is known of him, were brought to me, I would strike him with the hadd (الحدّ)—the hadd of the free person (حدّ الحرّ)—except for one lash." And from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever slanders a slave (مملوك) is subject to discretionary punishment (عزّر) due to the sanctity of Islam."

And from him (peace be upon him), concerning a free person (الحرّ) who slanders a slave (المملوك). He said: "He is questioned. If the slave's mother was a free woman (حرّة), the hadd (الحدّ) is applied."

And from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) ruled that slander (الفرية) is of three types (ثلاث وجوه): when a man accuses another man of fornication (الزّنى), when he says his mother is an adulteress (زانية), and when he calls him by a name other than his father's. In these cases, the hadd is eighty lashes."

And from him (peace be upon him), concerning a man who said to another man, "O son of the doer (يا بن الفاعلة)," meaning fornication (الزّنا). He said: "If his mother is alive and present, and she comes seeking her right (حقّها), he is flogged eighty lashes. If she is absent, she is waited for until she returns and seeks her right. If she has died and nothing but good was known of her, the slanderer (المفتري) is struck with the hadd (الحدّ) of eighty lashes."

And from him (peace be upon him), he said: "If a man accuses another man and says, 'You commit the act of the people of Lot (قوم لوط), you have intercourse with men,' he is flogged with the hadd (حدّ) of the accuser, eighty lashes."

And from him (peace be upon him), he said that Ali (peace be upon him) used to say: "If a man says to another man, 'O ma'fūj (يا معفوج)' or 'O one who is sodomized in his rear (يا منكوحاً في دبره),' then the hadd (الحدّ) of the accuser is due upon him."

I say: Al-'Afj (العفج), with the undotted letter (المهملة), the fa' (الفاء), and the jim (الجيم), means sexual intercourse (الجماع).

And from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who slandered a group of people (قوم جماعة). He said: "If they are brought to him collectively (مجتمعين), he is struck with one hadd (حدّاً واحداً). But if they are brought to him separately (متفرّقين), he is struck with a hadd for each one of them."

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), concerning a man who accuses an entire group (القوم جميعاً) with a single word. He said: "If he does not name them, then only one hadd (حدّ واحد) is due upon him. But if he names them, then a hadd is due upon him for every man."

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said that the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that nothing of the accuser's clothes should be removed except the outer garment (الرداء)."

And from him (peace be upon him), he said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The fornicator (الزاني) is struck harder than the wine drinker (شارب الخمر), and the wine drinker is struck harder than the accuser (القاذف), and the accuser is struck harder than the discretionary punishment (التعزير)."

And from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "The slanderer (المفتري) is flogged with a striking that is between the two strikings (ضرباً بين الضّربين); his entire body is struck."

وَلا تَقْبَلُوا لَهُمْ شَهادَةً أَبَداً وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ. (4) and never accept any testimony from them, and it is they who are the transgressors.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And the verse وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَناتِ (And those who accuse chaste women) was revealed in Medina." He said: "Allah cleared him (the slanderer) of being called a believer (الايمان) as long as he persists in the slander (الفرية). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: أَفَمَنْ كانَ مُؤْمِناً كَمَنْ كانَ فاسِقاً لا يَسْتَوُونَ (Is someone who is a believer like someone who is a transgressor? They are not equal). And Allah made him a hypocrite (منافقاً), so Allah said: إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ (Indeed, the hypocrites are the transgressors). And Allah made him among the allies of Iblis. He said: إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ كانَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَفَسَقَ (4) except Iblis, who was one of the jinn, and he defied عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِ (4) the command of his Lord. And He made him cursed, so He said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَناتِ الْغافِلاتِ الْمُؤْمِناتِ لُعِنُوا فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ يَوْمَ تَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ وَأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (23) Indeed, those who accuse chaste, unwary, faithful women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter, and there is a great punishment for them (24) on the day when their tongues, their hands, and their feet will bear witness against them concerning what they used to do. And the limbs (الجوارح) do not bear witness against a believer (مؤمن); rather, they bear witness against the one upon whom the word of punishment (كلمة العذاب) has been justly decreed. As for the believer, he will be given his book in his right hand. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَمَنْ أُوتِيَ كِتابَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ فَأُولئِكَ يَقْرَؤُنَ كِتابَهُمْ وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ فَتِيلاً. (71) As for him who is given his book in his right hand, they will read their book, and they will not be wronged so much as a thread.

إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تابُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (5) except those who repent after that and reform. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The slanderer (القاذف) is lashed eighty lashes (جلدات), and his testimony (شهادة) is never accepted except after repentance (التوبة) or if he recants his own statement (يكذّب نفسه). And if three testify and one refuses, the three are lashed, and their testimony is not accepted until four say: "We saw [it] like the kohl stick (الميل) in the kohl container (المكحلة)." And whoever testifies against himself that he committed adultery (زنى), his testimony is not accepted until he repeats it four times.

And in al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib: He (the Imam) was asked how his repentance (توبته) is known. He said: He recants his statement (يكذّب نفسه) publicly (على رؤوس الخلائق) when he is being lashed, and he seeks forgiveness from his Lord. If he does that, his repentance has become manifest.

And from him عليه السلام: He was asked about a man who slanders another man, is lashed the prescribed punishment (حدّاً), then repents, and nothing but good is known of him—is his testimony acceptable? He said: "Yes. What is said among you?" It was said: "They say his repentance is between him and Allah, but his testimony is never accepted." He said: "What a terrible thing they have said! My father used to say: If he repents and nothing but good is known of him, his testimony is acceptable."

(6) وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْواجَهُمْ وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ شُهَداءُ إِلَّا أَنْفُسُهُمْ فَشَهادَةُ أَحَدِهِمْ أَرْبَعُ شَهاداتٍ بِاللهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (6) And those who accuse their wives, and have no witnesses except themselves, the testimony of such a one shall be four testimonies [swearing] by Allah that he is indeed one of the truthful. And أَرْبَعُ شَهاداتٍ ** was recited with the nominative case (الرّفع). بِاللهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** meaning: regarding what he accused her of, namely adultery (الزّنا).

(7) وَالْخامِسَةُ أَنَّ لَعْنَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ ** (And the fifth [oath] shall be that Allah’s curse be upon him) It was recited with a softened inna (ان) إِنْ كانَ مِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ ** (if he is one of the liars) regarding the accusation.

(8) وَيَدْرَؤُا ** (But it shall avert) meaning, it shall repel عَنْهَا الْعَذابَ ** (the punishment from her)—meaning, stoning (الرجم)— **أَنْ تَشْهَدَ أَرْبَعَ شَهاداتٍ بِاللهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ ** (if she testifies four times by Allah that he is indeed one of the liars) in what he accused me of.

(9) وَالْخامِسَةُ ** (And the fifth [oath]) It was recited with the nasb case (بالنّصب) أَنَّ غَضَبَ اللهِ عَلَيْها إِنْ كانَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (shall be that Allah’s wrath be upon her if he is one of the truthful) in that matter. It was recited with a softened inna (انّ) and the kasrah on the ḍād (وكسر الضّاد).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and he said: "It refers to the accuser (القاذف) who accuses his wife. If he accuses her, and then admits that he lied against her, he is given the prescribed punishment (الحدّ), and his wife is returned to him. But if he insists on proceeding, he must testify against her four times by Allah that he is indeed one of the truthful, and the fifth [oath] is that he invokes the curse upon himself if he is one of the liars. If she wishes to avert the punishment from herself—and the punishment is stoning (الرّجم)—she must testify: أَرْبَعَ شَهاداتٍ بِاللهِ إِنَّهُ لَمِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ (four times by Allah that he is indeed one of the liars) وَالْخامِسَةَ أَنَّ غَضَبَ اللهِ عَلَيْها إِنْ كانَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ (and the fifth [oath] shall be that Allah’s wrath be upon her if he is one of the truthful). If she does not do this, she is stoned. If she does it, she averts the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) from herself, and then she is not lawful for him until the Day of Resurrection." It was asked: "What if they are separated and she has a child who dies?" He said: "His mother inherits him. If his mother dies, his maternal uncles (أخواله) inherit him. And whoever claims that he is a child of fornication (ولد زنا) is given the prescribed punishment (جلد الحدّ)." It was asked: "Is the child returned to him if he acknowledges him?" He said: "No, and no honor (ولا كرامة). The son does not inherit [the father], and the son inherits him [the father]."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man from the Muslims came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, tell me, if a man entered his house and found a man having intercourse with his wife, what should he do?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned away from him, and the man departed. That man was the one who had been afflicted by this situation with his wife. He said: Then the revelation (الوحي) descended from Allah concerning the ruling for both of them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent for that man and called him. He said to him: "Are you the one who saw a man with your wife?" He said: "Yes." He said to him: "Go and bring me your wife, for Allah has revealed the ruling concerning you and her." The husband brought her, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم made them stand. Then He said to the husband: "Testify four times by Allah that you are indeed one of the truthful in what you accused her of." He testified. Then He said to him: "Fear Allah, for the curse of Allah is severe." Then He said to him: "Testify the fifth [oath] that the curse of Allah be upon you if you are one of the liars." He testified. Then He commanded that he be moved aside. Then He said to the woman: "Testify four times by Allah that your husband is indeed one of the liars in what he accused you of." She testified. Then He said to her: "Stop," and He admonished her. He said to her: "Fear Allah, for the wrath of Allah is severe." Then He said to her: "Testify the fifth [oath] that the wrath of Allah be upon you if your husband is one of the truthful in what he accused you of." She testified. He said: Then He separated them and said to them: "Never reunite in marriage after you have performed li'an (تلاعنتما)."

And al-Qummi [narrated]: That it was revealed concerning al-li'an (اللّعان). The reason for this was that when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned from the Battle of Tabuk, Uwaymir ibn Sa'idah al-Ajlani (عويمر بن ساعدة العجلاني), who was one of the Ansar (الأنصار), came to Him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, my wife committed fornication with Sharik ibn Samha (شريك بن سمحا), and she is pregnant by him." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned away from him. He repeated his statement, and He turned away from him, until he did this four times. Then the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) entered His house, and the verse of al-li'an (آية اللعان) was revealed to Him. The Messenger of Allah went out and prayed the afternoon prayer (العصر) with the people, and He said to Uwaymir: "Bring me your wife, for Allah has revealed a Quranic text concerning the two of you." So he came to her. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to her: "He calls you." She was held in high esteem by her people, so a group of them came with her. When she entered the mosque, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to Uwaymir: "Step forward to the pulpit (المنبر) and perform li'an (التعنا)." He asked: "How should I do it?" He said: "Step forward and say: 'I bear witness by Allah that I am indeed one of the truthful in what I accused her of.'" So he stepped forward and said it. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Repeat it." He repeated it. Then He said: "Repeat it." He repeated it, until he did that four times. Then He said to him for the fifth [oath]: "The curse of Allah be upon you if you are one of the liars in what you accused her of." So he said for the fifth [oath]: "The curse of Allah be upon him if he is one of the liars in what he accused her of." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The curse is binding if you are a liar." Then He said to him: "Move aside," and he moved aside. Then He said to his wife: "Will you testify as he testified, or else I will carry out Allah's prescribed punishment (حدّ الله) upon you?" She looked at the faces of her people and said: "I will not blacken these faces this evening." So she stepped forward to the pulpit and said: "I bear witness by Allah that Uwaymir ibn Sa'idah is one of the liars in what he accused me of." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to her: "Repeat it." She repeated it until she repeated it four times. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to her: "Invoke the curse upon yourself (الْعَني نَفْسَكِ) for the fifth [oath] if he is one of the truthful in what he accused you of." So she said for the fifth [oath]: "The wrath of Allah be upon her if he is one of the truthful in what he accused me of." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Woe to you! It is binding upon you." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to her husband: "Go, for she shall never be lawful for you." He said: "O Messenger of Allah, what about the property (ما لي) that I gave her?" He said: "If you were a liar, then it is further removed from you than that [i.e., you have no right to it]. And if you were truthful, it is hers because you made her private parts lawful [for yourself]." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "If she gives birth to a child with thin shanks (احمش الساقين), large eyes (انفس العينين), and tightly curled hair (جَعْد قطط), then he belongs to the evil matter [i.e., the adulterer]. But if she gives birth to him with light-colored eyes (اشهل) and reddish hair (اصهب), then he belongs to his father." It is said that she gave birth to him according to the evil matter. Thus, she is not lawful for her husband. If she gives birth to a child, his father does not inherit him, and his inheritance belongs to his mother, and if he has no mother, then to his maternal uncles (أخواله). And if anyone accuses him, he is given the prescribed punishment for the accuser (حدّ القاذف).

In al-Awali, it is narrated: That Hilal ibn Umayyah (هلال بن اميّة) accused his wife of fornication with Sharik ibn al-Samha (شريك بن السّمحا). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Produce the evidence (البيّنة), or else there will be a prescribed punishment (حدّ) on your back." He said: "O Messenger of Allah, does one of us find a man with his wife and then seek evidence?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم kept saying: "Produce the evidence, or else there will be a prescribed punishment on your back." He said: "By Him Who sent You with the truth, I am indeed truthful, and Allah will surely reveal what will clear my back from the lashing (الجلد)." Then the Almighty's saying was revealed: **وَالَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ أَزْواجَهُمْ ** (And those who accuse their wives) the verse.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man accuses his wife, he does not perform li'an (يلاعنها) with her until he says: "I saw a man between her legs committing fornication with her."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Imam (الإِمام) sits facing away from the Qiblah (القبلة), and he makes her stand before him facing the Qiblah directly opposite him. He begins with the man, then the woman.

And in a narration: He places the man on his right and the woman on his left.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding a man whom the Imam (الإِمام) made stand for li'an, and he testified two oaths, then he retracted (نكل) and admitted he lied before completing the li'an. He said: "He is lashed with the lashing of the accuser (جلد القاذف), and separation is not made between him and his wife."

And narrated from Imam al-Jawad (peace be upon him): He was asked: "How is it that when a man accuses his wife, his testimony consists of four oaths by Allah, but if someone else accuses her—a father, brother, son, or relative—he is given the prescribed punishment (جلد الحدّ) and must produce evidence (البيّنة) for what he said?" He replied: "Ja'far (peace be upon him) was asked about that, and he said: 'If the husband accuses his wife and says, "I saw that with my own eyes," his testimony consists of four oaths by Allah. But if he says that he did not see it, he is told: "Produce evidence for what you said, or else you will be treated like anyone else." This is because Allah granted the husband access (مدخلاً) that He did not grant to others, neither father nor son, as he enters upon her by night and day. Thus, it is permissible for him to say, "I saw." But if someone else said, "I saw," he would be asked: "And what granted you access to the place where you saw this alone? You are accused, so the prescribed punishment that Allah has made obligatory upon you must be carried out." He said: "The husband's testimony became four oaths because of the place of the four witnesses (الأربعة شهداء), with an oath (يمين) in place of every witness.'"

In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked why four witnesses are required for fornication (الزّنا) and only two witnesses for murder (القتل). He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made al-mut'ah (المتعة) lawful for you, and He knew that it would be denied against you. So He made the four witnesses a precaution for you, for were it not for that, you would be ruined. And rarely do four witnesses gather for a single matter."

And in another narration, he (peace be upon him) said: "Fornication (الزّنا) involves two prescribed punishments (حدّان), and it is not permissible for every two [witnesses] to testify against one [person], because both the man and the woman incur the prescribed punishment (الالحدّ). As for murder (القتل), the prescribed punishment is only carried out upon the killer, and it is averted from the victim (المقتول)."

(10) **وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ وَأَنَّ اللهَ تَوَّابٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy, and that Allah is all-clement, all-wise [He would have hastened the punishment for you].) He would have exposed you and hastened the punishment upon you. The apodosis [of the conditional clause] was omitted to magnify [the consequence].

(11) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ جاؤُ بِالْإِفْكِ ** (Indeed those who brought the slander) — with the most extreme form of falsehood (بأبلغ ما يكون من الكذب) — عُصْبَةٌ مِنْكُمْ ** (are a faction among you) — a group (جماعة) from among you — لا تَحْسَبُوهُ شَرًّا لَكُمْ ** (Do not suppose it is bad for you) — this is a new sentence (استيناف), and the pronoun (الهاء) refers to the slander (الإِفك) — بَلْ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (rather it is good for you) — because you acquired great reward (الثواب العظيم) through it — لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ مَا اكْتَسَبَ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ ** (Everyone of them shall receive his share of the sin he has incurred) — according to the extent he indulged in it (بقدر ما خاض فيه) — وَالَّذِي تَوَلَّى كِبْرَهُ ** (and he who took on the main part of it) — its greatest portion (معظمه) — مِنْهُمْ ** (among them) — meaning, among those who indulged (من الخائضين) — لَهُ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ. (shall incur a great punishment.)

In al-Jawami': The reason for the slander (الإفك) was that 'A'ishah lost her necklace during the campaign of Banu al-Mustaliq. She had gone out to relieve herself (لقضاء حاجة) and returned seeking it. Her litter (هودجها) was carried upon her camel, as they thought she was inside it. When she returned to the place, she found that they had departed. Safwan was behind the army. When he reached that place and recognized her, he made his camel kneel until she mounted it, and he led it until he reached the army, who had stopped during the heat of midday (قائم الظّهيرة). He said: Thus did al-Zuhri narrate it from 'A'ishah.

And al-Qummi [said]: The general public (العامّة) narrated that it was revealed concerning 'A'ishah and what she was accused of during the campaign of Banu al-Mustaliq of Khuzā'ah. As for the select group (الخاصّة), they narrated that it was revealed concerning Mariyah the Copt (مارية القبطية) and what 'A'ishah accused her of.

Then it was narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: When Ibrahim, the son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, died, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم grieved over him with intense sorrow. 'A'ishah said to Him: "What is it that grieves You over him? He is nothing but the son of Jurayh." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent 'Ali (peace be upon him) and commanded him to kill him. 'Ali (peace be upon him) went to him with the sword. Jurayh the Copt (جريح القبطي) was in an enclosure (حائط). 'Ali knocked on the garden gate (باب البستان). Jurayh approached him to open the door, but when he saw 'Ali, he recognized the anger on his face, so he turned back and did not open the garden gate. 'Ali leaped over the wall and descended into the garden and followed him. Jurayh turned away fleeing. When he feared that 'Ali would overtake him, he climbed a date palm, and 'Ali climbed after him. When 'Ali drew near to him, he threw himself down from the top of the date palm, and his private parts became exposed. Behold, he had neither what men have nor what women have. 'Ali returned to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said to Him: "O Messenger of Allah, when You send me on a matter, should I be like a heated nail in wool (كالمسمار المحمي في الوبر), proceeding upon it, or should I verify (أثبّت)?" He said: "No, rather verify." 'Ali said: "By Him Who sent You with the truth, he has neither what men have nor what women have." The Prophet said: "All praise belongs to Allah Who averted evil from us, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." This narration was cited by al-Qummi with a different wording in Surat al-Hujurat regarding the Almighty's saying: إِنْ جاءَكُمْ فاسِقٌ بِنَبَإٍ فَتَبَيَّنُوا ** (If a transgressor brings you any news, verify it) and he added: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought him [Jurayh] and said to him: "What is your situation, O Jurayh?" He said: "O Messenger of Allah, the Copts love their attendants (حشمهم) and those who enter their families (أهاليهم), and the Copts do not feel comfortable (يأنسون) except with Copts. Her father sent me to enter upon her, serve her, and keep her company (أونسها)."

I say: If this report is authentic, then perhaps He only sent 'Ali to Jurayh to manifest the truth and avert the evil, and He already knew that 'Ali would not kill him. He would not have commanded his killing merely based on 'A'ishah's statement. This is supported by what al-Qummi narrated in Surat al-Hujurat from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded the killing of the Copt while knowing that she had lied against him, or if He did not know, and that Allah only averted [harm] from the Copt through 'Ali's (peace be upon him) verification (تثبت). He said: "Yes, by Allah, He did know. If the command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم had been a firm resolve (عزيمة) for killing, 'Ali would not have returned until he had killed him. However, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم only did that so that she might repent from her sin, but she did not repent, nor was the killing of a Muslim man due to her lie difficult for her."

(١٢) **لَوْ لا إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ ظَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتُ بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ خَيْراً وَقالُوا هذا إِفْكٌ مُبِينٌ ** (Why did not the believing men and women, when you heard it, think well of their own people, and say, 'This is a manifest lie'?)

As the certain one (المستيقن), who is aware of the situation (المطّلع على الحال), says. And He only shifted therein from direct address (الخطاب) to the third person (الغيبة) as an emphasis in clarification (التوضيح) and as an indication that faith necessitates thinking well of the believers, refraining from slandering them, and repelling those who slander them, just as they repel them from themselves.

(١٣) **لَوْ لا جاؤُ عَلَيْهِ بِأَرْبَعَةِ شُهَداءَ فَإِذْ لَمْ يَأْتُوا بِالشُّهَداءِ فَأُولئِكَ عِنْدَ اللهِ هُمُ الْكاذِبُونَ ** (13) Why did they not bring four witnesses to it? So when they did not bring the witnesses, they are indeed the liars in Allah’s sight.

It is said that [this Ayah] is a resumption (استيناف), or it is part of the preceding statement, confirming that [the accusation] is a lie. For indeed, that for which there is no evidence is deemed a lie in the sight of Allah, meaning, in His ruling (حكمه). And for that reason, the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) was established upon it.

(14) وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ ** (And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy in the world and the Hereafter) This لَوْ لَا (law lā) indicates the non-occurrence of a thing due to the existence of another. And the meaning is: And were it not for Allah's grace upon you in the world with various kinds of blessings, among which is the granting of success (الامهار) for repentance, and His mercy in the Hereafter through the pardon and forgiveness decreed for you, لَمَسَّكُمْ ** (a great punishment would have surely afflicted you) immediately فِيما أَفَضْتُمْ فِيهِ ** (for what you indulged in)—meaning, you plunged into it—عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (a great punishment), compared to which blame and lashing are insignificant.

(15) إِذْ تَلَقَّوْنَهُ بِأَلْسِنَتِكُمْ ** (15) when you received it with your tongues some of you taking it from others by asking about it, وَتَقُولُونَ بِأَفْواهِكُمْ ** (and you say with your mouths) without assistance from the hearts, ما لَيْسَ لَكُمْ بِهِ عِلْمٌ وَتَحْسَبُونَهُ هَيِّناً ** (what you have no knowledge of, and you deem it light), easy, having no consequence, وَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللهِ عَظِيمٌ ** and it was momentous with Allah. in sin (الوزر) and the intensity (استحرار) of the punishment (العذاب). So these are three consecutive sins (آثام مترتبة) to which the touch (مسّ) of the great punishment (العذاب العظيم) is attached.

(16) وَلَوْ لا إِذْ سَمِعْتُمُوهُ قُلْتُمْ ما يَكُونُ لَنا ** (16) Why did you not say, when you heard it, 'It is not for us) (It is not) what is proper (ما ينبغي) and what is right (وما يصحّ) for us أَنْ نَتَكَلَّمَ بِهذا سُبْحانَكَ ** (to speak of this. Glory be to You!) It is astonishment (تعجّب) at whoever says that, for Allah is glorified (يُنَزَّه) by every astonished person from the possibility that it might be difficult for Him, or it is a glorification (تنزيه) of Allah from the possibility that the sanctity (حرمة) of His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم could be immoral (فاجرة), for her immorality (فجورها) is a repulsion (تنفير) from him, unlike her disbelief (كفرها). هذا بُهْتانٌ عَظِيمٌ ** (This is a monstrous slander.') Due to the greatness of the one slandered (المبهوت عليه).

(١٧) يَعِظُكُمُ اللهُ أَنْ تَعُودُوا لِمِثْلِهِ أَبَداً إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (17) Allah advises you never to return to the like of it, should you be faithful. for indeed faith prevents it, and in this [Ayah] there is incitement (تهييج) and reprimand (تقريع).

(١٨) وَيُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الْآياتِ ** (18) Allah clarifies the signs for you, the signs indicating the laws (الشرايع) and the virtues of manners (محاسن الآداب), so that you may be admonished and disciplined. وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ ** and Allah is all-knowing, of all circumstances, Wise (حَكِيمٌ) in His management (تدابيره).

(١٩) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ أَنْ تَشِيعَ الْفاحِشَةُ فِي الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ. (19) Indeed, those who like indecency to spread among the faithful shall have a painful punishment in the world and the Hereafter, and Allah knows and you do not know.

In al-Kafi, al-Amali, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever speaks concerning a believer what his eyes have seen and his ears have heard, he is among those concerning whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ ** (Indeed, those who like...)—the Ayah."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "A man from my brethren—something I dislike has reached me concerning him, so I ask him about it and he denies it, yet trustworthy people have informed me about him." He (peace be upon him) said: "Belie your hearing and your sight concerning your brother, and even if fifty oath-takers (قسامة) testify against him before you, and he tells you a statement, then believe him and disbelieve them. And do not spread anything about him by which you disgrace him and destroy his chivalry (مروّته), lest you become one of those concerning whom Allah the Almighty said: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُحِبُّونَ ** (Indeed, those who like...)—the Ayah."

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever spreads indecency is like the one who initiated it."

The Almighty's saying: (٢٠) وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ ** (20) And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy A repetition of the favor (المنّة) to emphasize the avoidance of swift punishment (المعاجلة بالعقاب), indicating the enormity of the crime. The answer (to لَوْ لا) was omitted because it was sufficient having been mentioned once before. وَأَنَّ اللهَ رَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ ** And that Allah is All-kind, All-merciful Since He did not hasten the punishment upon you.

(٢١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ ** (21) O you who have faith! Do not follow in the footsteps of Satan. (This refers to) spreading indecency (الفاحشة).

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): (خطأت) with the hamzah (is read).

وَمَنْ يَتَّبِعْ خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ فَإِنَّهُ يَأْمُرُ بِالْفَحْشاءِ وَالْمُنْكَرِ ** And whoever follows in the footsteps of Satan, he indeed bids to indecency and transgression. الفحشاء (indecency) is that which is excessive in its ugliness, and المنكر (transgression) is that which the Divine Law (الشرع) and the intellect (العقل) reject.

وَلَوْ لا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُ ** And were it not for Allah’s grace upon you and His mercy, (This grace and mercy is realized) by granting success (توفيق) for repentance (التوبة) which wipes away sins (الذنوب), and by legislating the prescribed punishments (الحدود) which atone for them,

ما زَكى ** not one of you would ever have been purified, (Meaning) not one of you would have been purified (ما طهر) from its filth (دنسها) مِنْكُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ أَبَداً (from among you) ever, until the end of time (اخر الدّهر).

وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يُزَكِّي مَنْ يَشاءُ ** but Allah purifies whomever He wishes. (This is achieved) by inclining him toward repentance (التوبة) and accepting it.

وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ ** and Allah is All-hearing, (He is All-hearing) of their speech (لمقالتهم),

عَلِيمٌ ** All-knowing. (He is All-knowing) of their intentions (بنيّاتهم).

(٢٢) وَلا يَأْتَلِ ** And let him not swear (يَحْلِفْ) from al-Iliyyah (الاليّة) on the measure (وزن) of fa'liyyah (فعلية) meaning the oath (اليمين), or let him not fall short (يُقَصِّرْ) from al-Alw (الالْو).

أُولُوا الْفَضْلِ ** The wealthy (الغني).

مِنْكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ ** in wealth (في المال).

**أَنْ يُؤْتُوا أُولِي الْقُرْبى وَالْمَساكِينَ وَالْمُهاجِرِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ **

In al-Jawami', it is said that it was revealed concerning a group of the Companions (الصحابة) who swore that they would not give charity (يتصدّقوا) to those who spoke anything of the slander (الافك) nor would they assist them (يواسوهم).

**وَلا يَأْتَلِ أُولُوا الْفَضْلِ مِنْكُمْ وَالسَّعَةِ أَنْ يُؤْتُوا أُولِي الْقُرْبى وَالْمَساكِينَ وَالْمُهاجِرِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَلْيَعْفُوا وَلْيَصْفَحُوا أَلا تُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللهُ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (22) Let those of you who are favoured and well-off not swear that they will not give to the relatives and the needy and the emigrants in the way of Allah. Let them pardon and forbear. Do you not desire that Allah should forgive you? And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Ulu al-Qurba (أولو القربى) are the relatives (قرابة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He says: Let some of you pardon others, and let some of you forbear towards others. If you do this, it will be a mercy from Allah for you. Allah says: **أَلا تُحِبُّونَ ** (Do you not desire) the Ayah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: (It was recited) وَلْتَعْفُوا وَلْتَصْفَحُوا (and let them pardon and let them forbear) with the tā' (بالتّاء), just as it was also narrated with the yā' (بالياء).

And in al-Manaqib, what preceded during the exegesis of وَلَدَيْنا كِتابٌ يَنْطِقُ بِالْحَقِ ** (And with Us is a Book that speaks the truth) from Surat al-Mu'minun (سورة المؤمنين).

(٢٣) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَرْمُونَ الْمُحْصَناتِ الْغافِلاتِ ** of what they have been accused of, [who are] the believers (الْمُؤْمِناتِ) in Allah and His Messenger. لُعِنُوا فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ ** just as they slandered them, وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ due to the magnitude of their sins. (23) Indeed, those who accuse chaste, unwary women are cursed in this world and the Hereafter, and there is a great punishment for them.

(٢٤) يَوْمَ تَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** It was read with the yā’ (ي) [i.e., يَشْهَدُ]. أَلْسِنَتُهُمْ وَأَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** by Allah making them speak, without their choice. (24) on the day when their tongues and their hands and their feet will bear witness against them concerning what they used to do.

(٢٥) يَوْمَئِذٍ يُوَفِّيهِمُ اللهُ دِينَهُمُ الْحَقَ ** their deserved reward, وَيَعْلَمُونَ ** due to their witnessing the matter, **أَنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْحَقُّ الْمُبِينُ ** the Just, the Manifest, in Whose judgment there is no injustice. (25) On that day Allah will pay them their due recompense in full, and they will know that Allah is the manifest Truth.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The limbs do not bear witness against a believer (مؤمن); rather, they bear witness against the one upon whom the word of punishment (كلمة العذاب) has been justly decreed. The complete narration has already passed in this surah.

(٢٦) الْخَبِيثاتُ لِلْخَبِيثِينَ وَالْخَبِيثُونَ لِلْخَبِيثاتِ وَالطَّيِّباتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ وَالطَّيِّبُونَ لِلطَّيِّباتِ (26) Vicious women are for vicious men, and vicious men are for vicious women, and good women are for good men, and good men are for good women.

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): The vicious women (الخبيثات من النساء) are for the vicious men (الخبيثين من الرجال), and the vicious men (الخبيثون من الرجال) are for the vicious women (الخبيثات من النساء), and the good women (الطيبات من النساء) are for the good men (الطيبين من الرّجال), and the good men (الطيبون من الرجال) are for the good women (الطيبات من النساء). They both said: It is like His saying: **الزَّانِي لا يَنْكِحُ إِلَّا زانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً ** (The fornicator shall marry none but a fornicatress or an idolatress). Except that some people intended to marry them (the vicious women), so Allah forbade them from that and disliked that for them.

And it was said that the vicious women (الخبيثات) and the good women (الطيبات) refer to sayings (الأقوال) and words (الكلم). And al-Qummi says: The vicious things (الخبيثات) of speech and action are for the vicious men and women (الخبيثين من الرجال والنساء); they accept them (يسلّمونهم) and confirm the one who said them (يصدّق عليهم من قال). And the good men and women (الطيبون من الرجال والنساء) are for the good things (الطيبات) of speech and action. That which is close to this has already passed in Surat al-Anfal in the exegesis of this verse.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Hasan al-Mujtaba (peace be upon him), when he stood up from the gathering of Mu'awiyah and his companions, having silenced them (وقد القمهم الحجر): الْخَبِيثاتُ لِلْخَبِيثِينَ وَالْخَبِيثُونَ لِلْخَبِيثاتِ ** (Vicious women are for vicious men, and vicious men are for vicious women) — by Allah, O Mu'awiyah, they are you and these companions of yours and your followers (شيعتك). وَالطَّيِّباتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ ** (and good women are for good men) — until the end of the Ayah — they are Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and his companions and his followers (شيعته).

أُولئِكَ ** (These) — meaning the good men (الطيبين), and for the good women (الطيبات) according to the first interpretation, and the good men (الطيبين) according to the latter interpretation — مُبَرَّؤُنَ مِمَّا يَقُولُونَ ** (are absolved of what they say) concerning them, or from saying the like of their saying. لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَرِزْقٌ كَرِيمٌ (For them is forgiveness and a noble provision).

(27) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتاً غَيْرَ بُيُوتِكُمْ (O you who have faith! Do not enter houses other than your own houses) which you inhabit, حَتَّى تَسْتَأْنِسُوا (until you seek permission) (meaning) you seek permission (تَسْتَأْذِنُوا) from al-isti'nās (الاستيناس), which means seeking information (الاستعلام), derived from (أَنِسَ) the thing when he perceived it (أَبْصَرَهُ). For the one seeking permission (المستأذن) is seeking information about the situation (مستعلم للحال), investigating whether his entry is desired. Or (it is derived) from al-isti'nās (الاستيناس) which is the opposite of feeling alienated (الاستيحاش), for the one seeking permission (المستأذن) is feeling alienated (مستوحش), fearing that he will not be granted permission, وَتُسَلِّمُوا عَلى أَهْلِها (and greet their inhabitants) by saying: "السلام عليكم" (Peace be upon you), "أَأَدْخُل" (May I enter?).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): A man sought permission to enter upon him and cleared his throat (فَتَنَحْنَحَ). So, he said to a woman called Rawḍah (روضة): "Go to this man and teach him, and tell him to say: 'السلام عليكم أَأَدْخُل' (Peace be upon you, may I enter?)." The man heard her and said it, so he (the Prophet) said: "Enter."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about al-isti'nās (الاستيناس). He replied: "The man speaks the tasbīḥah (التسبيحة), the taḥmīdah (التحميدة), and the takbīrah (التكبيرة), and clears his throat (يَتَنَحْنَحُ) for the inhabitants of the house (أَهْلِ الْبَيْتِ)."

In al-Ma'ani and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "Al-isti'nās (الاستيناس) is the sound of the sandal (وَقْعُ النَّعْلِ) and the greeting (التسليم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man seeks permission when he enters upon his father, but the father does not seek permission upon the son. And a man seeks permission upon his daughter and his sister if they are married.

In al-Majma': A man said to the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): "Should I seek permission upon my mother?" He said: "Yes." The man said: "She has no servant other than me. Should I seek permission upon her every time I enter?" He said: "Do you like to see her naked?" The man said: "No." He said: "Then seek permission upon her."

In al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Permission (الإذن) is only for the houses (البيوت), not for the courtyard (الدَّار).

ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (That is better for you) meaning, seeking permission (الاستيذان) and greeting (التسليم) is better for you than entering suddenly (بَغْتَةً), لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (so that you may take admonition). This was said to you with the intention that you may take admonition (تَذَكَّرُوا) and know what is most beneficial (أَصْلَح) for you.

(٢٨) فَإِنْ لَمْ تَجِدُوا فِيها أَحَداً ** who permits you, فَلا تَدْخُلُوها حَتَّى يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ وَإِنْ قِيلَ لَكُمُ ارْجِعُوا فَارْجِعُوا ** and do not insist, هُوَ أَزْكى لَكُمْ وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ (28) If you do not find anyone in it, do not enter them until you are permitted. And if you are told, 'Go back,' then go back. That is purer for you, and Allah knows best what you do.

(٢٩) لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتاً غَيْرَ مَسْكُونَةٍ فِيها مَتاعٌ لَكُمْ ** (There is no sin upon you in entering non-residential houses where you have some goods.) enjoyment (استمتاع) for you, such as seeking shelter (كالاستكنان) from heat and cold, accommodating men (وإيواء الرجال), and sitting for transactions (والجلوس للمعاملة). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: They are the public baths (الحمّامات), the inns (الخانات), and the mills (الأرحية) which you may enter without permission (بغير اذن). وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما تُبْدُونَ وَما تَكْتُمُونَ ** (and Allah knows what you disclose and what you conceal.) A threat (وعيد) to whoever enters a place (مدخلاً) for corruption (لفساد) or to look upon a private part (أو تطلّع على عورة).

(٣٠) قُلْ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَغُضُّوا مِنْ أَبْصارِهِمْ ** (30) Tell the faithful men to cast down their eyes meaning, that which is towards the unlawful (مُحَرَّم). وَيَحْفَظُوا فُرُوجَهُمْ ** and guard their private parts meaning, from the unlawful gaze (النَّظَر المُحَرَّم). ذلِكَ أَزْكى لَهُمْ ** That is purer for them purer (أَطْهَر) because it involves distance from suspicion (الرِّيبَة). إِنَّ اللهَ خَبِيرٌ بِما يَصْنَعُونَ** (30) Tell the faithful men to cast down their eyes and guard their private parts. That is purer for them. Indeed Allah is well aware of what they do.

(٣١) وَقُلْ لِلْمُؤْمِناتِ يَغْضُضْنَ مِنْ أَبْصارِهِنَّ وَيَحْفَظْنَ فُرُوجَهُنَ. (31) And tell the believing women to cast down their glances and guard their private parts.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Every verse in the Qur’an mentioning private parts (الفروج) refers to fornication (الزنا), except for this verse, for it refers to looking (النظر). Thus, it is not lawful for a believing man to look at the private part of his brother, nor is it lawful for a woman to look at the private part of her sister.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), in a hadith mentioning the obligation of faith upon the limbs (الجوارح): "And it is obligated upon the sight (البصر) not to look at what Allah has forbidden him, and to turn away from what Allah has prohibited, which is not lawful for him, and this is part of faith." Then He, the Blessed and Exalted, said: قُلْ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَغُضُّوا مِنْ أَبْصارِهِمْ وَيَحْفَظُوا فُرُوجَهُمْ ** (Tell the believing men to cast down their glances and guard their private parts). So, He forbade them from looking at their nakedness (عوراتهم), and that a person should look at the private part of his brother, and that he should guard his private part from being looked at. And He said: وَقُلْ لِلْمُؤْمِناتِ يَغْضُضْنَ مِنْ أَبْصارِهِنَّ وَيَحْفَظْنَ فُرُوجَهُنَ ** (And tell the believing women to cast down their glances and guard their private parts), meaning: from one of them looking at the private part of her sister, and that she should guard her private part from being looked at. And He said: Everything in the Qur’an concerning guarding the private part (حفظ الفرج) refers to fornication (الزنا), except for this verse, for it refers to looking (النظر).

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: A young man from the Ansar encountered a woman in Medina. The women used to cover their heads (يتقنّعن) behind their ears. He looked at her as she approached. When she passed, he looked at her again and entered an alleyway—which he named, belonging to Banu so-and-so—and began looking behind her. A bone in the wall or a piece of glass struck his face and split it. When the woman had passed, he looked and saw blood flowing onto his garment and chest. He said: "By Allah, I shall go to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and inform him!" He said: So he went to him. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saw him, He asked him: "What is this?" So he informed Him. Then Gabriel descended with this verse: وَلا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها. (and not display their adornment except what is manifest of it).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Almighty: إِلَّا ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها ** (except what is manifest of it), he said: The manifest adornment (الزينة الظاهرة) is kohl (الكحل) and the ring (الخاتم). And in a narration: The ring (الخاتم) and the muskah (المسكة), which is the bracelet (القلب).

I say: Al-Qalb (القلب) with a dammah (on the qaf) is the bracelet (السوار).

In al-Jawami', narrated from them (peace be upon them): The two palms (الكفّان) and the fingers (الأصابع).

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: It is the garments (الثياب), kohl (الكحل), the ring (الخاتم), henna on the palm (خضاب الكفّ), and the bracelet (السوار). And adornment (الزينة) is of three types: adornment for people (زينة للنّاس), adornment for the mahram (زينة للمحرم), and adornment for the husband (زينة للزّوج). As for the adornment for people, we have mentioned it. As for the adornment for the mahram, it is the place of the necklace and above it, the armlet (الدّملج) and below it, and the anklet (الخلخال) and below it. As for the adornment for the husband, it is the entire body.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: For the husband is what is beneath the shift (الدرع), and for the son and the brother is what is above the shift. And for the non-mahram (غير ذي محرم) are four garments: a shift (درع), a head-covering (خمار), an outer garment (جلباب), and a wrapper (إزار).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what is lawful for a man to see of a woman if he is not a mahram. He said: The face (الوجه), the two palms (الكفّان), and the two feet (القدمان). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no harm in looking at the heads of the people of Tihamah, the Bedouins (الأعراب), the people of al-Sawad (أهل السّواد), and the non-Arabs (العلوج), because if they are forbidden, they do not desist. He said: And the insane woman (المجنونة) and the one whose intellect is overcome (المغلوب على عقلها); there is no harm in looking at her hair and her body, as long as one does not intend it deliberately.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: There is no sanctity (لا حرمة) for the women of the People of the Covenant (أهل الذمّة) that one should look at their hair and their hands. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who wishes to marry a woman, whether he may examine her and look at her back and her face. He said: There is no harm. And in a narration: There is no harm in looking at her face and her wrists (معاصمها) if he intends to marry her.

I say: Al-Mi'sam (المعصم), like (كمنبر), with a kasrah on the mim, is the place of the bracelet (السوار). And in another narration: He may look at her hair and her beauties (محاسنها) if he is not seeking pleasure (متلذّذاً). And in another [narration]: He is only buying her for the highest price.

In al-Khisal, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "O Ali, the first glance is for you, but the second is against you, not for you." And in a narration: "The first glance at a woman is for you, so do not follow it up with another glance, and beware of temptation (الفتنة)." وَلْيَضْرِبْنَ بِخُمُرِهِنَّ عَلى جُيُوبِهِنَ ** (and let them draw their head-coverings over their bosoms), meaning: covering their necks (أعناقهنّ). وَلا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَ ** (and not display their adornment)—He repeated this to clarify for whom display is lawful and for whom it is not lawful—إِلَّا لِبُعُولَتِهِنَ ** (except for their husbands). For they are the intended recipients of the adornment, and they are permitted to look at their entire bodies, as previously mentioned. أَوْ آبائِهِنَّ أَوْ آباءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْنائِهِنَّ أَوْ أَبْناءِ بُعُولَتِهِنَّ أَوْ إِخْوانِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِي إِخْوانِهِنَّ أَوْ بَنِي أَخَواتِهِنَ ** (or their fathers, or their husbands’ fathers, or their sons, or their husbands’ sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or their sisters’ sons). What they are permitted to look at of them has already preceded.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the forearms (الذراعين) of a woman, whether they are part of the adornment about which Allah the Almighty said: وَلا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا لِبُعُولَتِهِنَ ** (and not display their adornment except for their husbands). He said: Yes, and what is beneath the head-covering (الخمار) is adornment, and what is beneath the two bracelets (السوارين).

أَوْ نِسائِهِنَ ** (or their women), meaning: the believing women (النساء المؤمنات).

In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: It is not proper for a woman to uncover herself before a Jewish or Christian woman, for they describe that to their husbands. أَوْ ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُنَ ** (or those whom their right hands own), meaning: male slaves (العبيد) and female slaves (الإماء). This is according to [the narration].

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: There is no harm if the owned person (المملوك) sees the hair and the shin (الساق). And in a narration: the hair of his mistress (مولاته) and her shin. And in another: There is no harm in him looking at her hair if he is trustworthy (مأموناً).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is not lawful for a woman that her slave (عبدها) should look at anything of her body except her hair, provided he does not intend it deliberately.

أَوِ التَّابِعِينَ غَيْرِ أُولِي الْإِرْبَةِ ** (or male attendants having no sexual desire), meaning: those who have no need (الحاجة) for women. And al-irbah (الإربة) is intellect (العقل) and soundness of opinion (جودة الرأي). And ghayr (غير) was recited with the nasb (accusative case). مِنَ الرِّجالِ.** (among men).

Al-Qummi [said]: He is the decrepit old man (الشيخ الفاني) who has no need for women.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: He is the fool (الأحمق) who does not approach women. And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The fool (الأحمق) who is under guardianship (المولّى عليه) who does not approach women.

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The attendant (التابع) is the one who follows you to obtain some of your food, and he has no need for women; he is the simpleton (الأبله) who is under guardianship.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who has a eunuch (الخصّى) who enters upon his women and hands them water for ablution (الوضوء), and thus sees their hair. He said: No. أَوِ الطِّفْلِ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يَظْهَرُوا عَلى عَوْراتِ النِّساءِ ** (or children who are not yet conscious of women’s nakedness), due to their lack of discernment (تميّزهم)—from al-zuhur (الظهور) meaning awareness (الاطلاع)—or due to their not having reached the limit of desire (حدّ الشهوة)—from al-zuhur (الظهور) meaning dominance (الغلبة). وَلا يَضْرِبْنَ بِأَرْجُلِهِنَّ لِيُعْلَمَ ما يُخْفِينَ مِنْ زِينَتِهِنَ ** (And let them not stamp their feet so that the adornment they hide may be known), so that her anklet (خلخالها) rattles, making it known that she possesses an anklet, for that causes inclination (ميلاً) in men. وَتُوبُوا إِلَى اللهِ جَمِيعاً أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (And repent to Allah, all of you, O believers), since scarcely anyone among you is free from negligence (تفريط), especially in restraining desires (الكفّ عن الشهوات). And ayyuh (أيّه) was recited with a dammah on the ha. لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ** (so that you may be felicitous), by attaining happiness in both abodes (بسعادة الدّارين).

(٣٢) وَأَنْكِحُوا الْأَيامى مِنْكُمْ ** (Marry off the single among you) It is the inverted form (مقلوب) of أيايم (ayāyim), the plural (جمع) of أيم (ayyim), which is the unmarried person (العزب), whether male or female, virgin or previously married (ثيّبا). وَالصَّالِحِينَ مِنْ عِبادِكُمْ وَإِمائِكُمْ ** (and the righteous among your slaves and your slave-girls.) It was said that He specified the righteous (الصَّالِحِينَ) because protecting their religion (إحصان دينهم) is more important (أهم). And it was said that the intended meaning (المراد) is rather those who are suitable for marriage (الصالحون للنّكاح). إِنْ يَكُونُوا فُقَراءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His grace.) This is a refutation of what might prevent marriage. وَاللهُ واسِعٌ (And Allah is All-bounteous,) possessing abundance (ذو سعة) whose blessings (نعمه) are inexhaustible (لا تنفد). عَلِيمٌ ** (All-knowing.) He expands the provision (يبسط الرزق) and restricts it (ويقدر) according to what His wisdom (حكمته) necessitates.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever abandons marriage (التزويج) fearing poverty (العيلة) has thought ill (أساء ظنّه) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: إِنْ يَكُونُوا فُقَراءَ يُغْنِهِمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ. (If they are poor, Allah will enrich them out of His grace.)"

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and complained to him of need (الحاجة). He said: "Marry," and He expanded (provision) for him.

** (٣٣) وَلْيَسْتَعْفِفِ الَّذِينَ لا يَجِدُونَ نِكاحاً ** (33) Let those who do not find the means to marry maintain chastity meaning: its causes (أسبابه). حَتَّى يُغْنِيَهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (until Allah enriches them out of His grace.) The well-known interpretation of this is that they should strive to suppress lust and seek chastity through spiritual discipline (الرياضة) in order to calm their desire. As the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O company of youth, whoever among you can afford the means (الباءة) should marry, and whoever cannot afford it, let him fast, for fasting is a shield (وجاء) for him."

I say: الباءة (al-Bā’ah) means intercourse (الجماع), and الوجاء (al-Wajā’) is when the testicles of the male are severely crushed (تُرضَّ) such that the desire for intercourse is removed. [The Prophet] intended that fasting cuts off marriage (النكاح) just as الوجاء cuts it off. It was said that the first verse [referring to the previous verse 32] was revealed to prohibit rejecting a believer and abandoning the marriage of a believing woman, and the second [verse 33] was for commanding the poor person to be patient in abandoning marriage, fearing the hardship (تعب) of the state of marriage, so there is no contradiction.

I say: Rather, it is better to interpret the first [verse] as a general prohibition against abandoning marriage for fear of subsequent poverty, as indicated by the narration concerning the fear of destitution (العيلة), and to interpret the second [verse] as a command for seeking chastity (الاستعفاف) specifically due to present, prohibitive poverty.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the second verse, He said: "They marry حَتَّى يُغْنِيَهُمُ اللهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (until Allah enriches them out of His grace)." Perhaps its meaning is that they seek chastity (العفّة) through marriage (التزويج) and protection (الإحصان) so that they become rich. Based on this, the two verses are consistent in meaning, except that this interpretation does not suit the phrase "do not find the means" (عدم الوجدان) except with difficulty (تكلّف). It is possible that the particle لَـ was dropped from the beginning of the narration, and the knowledge is with Allah.

وَالَّذِينَ يَبْتَغُونَ الْكِتابَ ** (And those who seek a deed of manumission) meaning المكاتبة (al-Mukātabah), which is when a man says to his slave: "I have contracted كتابة with you for such-and-such," meaning: "I have written upon myself your manumission (عتقك) if you pay such-and-such amount of money." مِمَّا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ ** (whom your right hands possess) whether a male slave (عبداً) or a female slave (أمة). فَكاتِبُوهُمْ إِنْ عَلِمْتُمْ فِيهِمْ خَيْراً. (write it for them if you know any good in them.)

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If you know that they possess wealth (مالاً)." And in another narration: "Debt (ديناً) and wealth (مالاً)."

In al-Faqih, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "And the good (الخير) is that he testifies that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and that he has a job (عمل) in his hand by which he earns, or that he has a craft (حرفة)." In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He was asked about a slave whose master contracts كتابة with him while knowing that the slave possesses neither little nor much. He said: "He should contract كتابة with him, even if he has to beg people, and he should not be prevented from المكاتبة because he has no wealth, for Allah provides sustenance to His servants, some from others, and the believer is aided (معان)."

وَآتُوهُمْ مِنْ مالِ اللهِ الَّذِي آتاكُمْ ** (and give them something out of Allah’s wealth which He has given you.) meaning: Give them something from what you contracted كتابة with them for.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "You reduce [the amount] from his installments (نجومه) which you did not intend to decrease, nor increase beyond what is in your soul." He was asked: "How much?" He replied: "Abu Ja‘far [Imam al-Baqir] reduced one thousand from six thousand for a slave."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Do not say, 'I will contract كتابة with him for five thousand and leave him one thousand,' but rather look at what you intended (أضمرت عليه) and give it to him."

وَلا تُكْرِهُوا فَتَياتِكُمْ عَلَى الْبِغاءِ ** (Do not compel your slave-girls into prostitution) meaning: into fornication (الزنا). إِنْ أَرَدْنَ تَحَصُّناً (if they desire to live in chastity) meaning: seeking chastity (تعفّفاً). [This condition] is a condition for compulsion (الإِكراه), for compulsion does not occur without it. And if it is made a condition for the prohibition (النّهي), the permissibility of compulsion (جواز الإكراه) does not necessarily follow from the absence of the condition, because the lifting of the prohibition might be due to the cessation of the prohibited act. لِتَبْتَغُوا عَرَضَ الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا.** (seeking the transient goods of the life of the world.)

Al-Qummi [said]: The Arabs and Quraysh used to buy [slave-girls] and impose heavy taxes (الضّريبة الثقيلة) upon them, saying: "Go and commit fornication (ازنوا) and earn [money]." So Allah prohibited them from that. وَمَنْ يُكْرِهْهُنَّ فَإِنَّ اللهَ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِكْراهِهِنَّ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** (And if someone compels them, Allah is indeed Forgiving and Merciful after their compulsion.) And it was recited: مِنْ بَعْدِ إِكْراهِهِنَ لهن غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ.** (after their compulsion, for them, [He is] Forgiving, Merciful.)

And [the reading] was attributed in al-Majma' to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). Al-Qummi [said]: Meaning, Allah will not hold them accountable for that if they were compelled to do it.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This verse is abrogated (منسوخة). It was abrogated by: فَإِنْ أَتَيْنَ بِفاحِشَةٍ فَعَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ ما عَلَى الْمُحْصَناتِ مِنَ الْعَذابِ. (If they commit an indecency, they shall be liable to half the penalty of free married women.)

(34) وَلَقَدْ أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكُمْ آياتٍ مُبَيِّناتٍ وَمَثَلاً (34) Certainly We have sent down to you manifest signs, and a parable And a wondrous story مِنَ الَّذِينَ from the likes of those who خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ وَمَوْعِظَةً لِلْمُتَّقِينَ from those who passed away before you, and an admonition for the Godwary. He specified them with it because they are the ones who benefit (المنتفعون).

(٣٥) اللهُ نُورُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth.) Manifest by Himself, the One Who makes them (the heavens and the earth) manifest with what is within them. In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He is the Guide (هاد) for the inhabitants of the heavens and the Guide (هاد) for the inhabitants of the earth. He said, and in the narration of al-Barqi: He is the guidance (هدى) for those in the heavens and the guidance (هدى) for those in the earth. مَثَلُ نُورِهِ (The parable of His Light) The description of His Light, which is wondrous in its affair, كَمِشْكاةٍ (is that of a niche) Like the description of a niche (مِشْكاة), which is a non-penetrating recess (a niche that does not go all the way through the wall). فِيها مِصْباحٌ (wherein there is a lamp.) A large, piercing lamp (سراج). الْمِصْباحُ فِي زُجاجَةٍ (The lamp is in a glass) In a lantern (قنديل) made of glass. الزُّجاجَةُ كَأَنَّها كَوْكَبٌ دُرِّيٌّ (The glass is as if it were a brilliant star) Luminous, glittering, attributed to the pearl (الدر). It was recited with a hamza (دُرِّيٌّ), and with the damma on the dal and the kasra on it, derived from al-Dar' (الدّرء), as if it repels darkness with its light. يُوقَدُ (lit) The lamp (المصباح) (is lit). It was recited with the ta' (تُوقَدُ), attributing it to the glass (الزجاجة) by omitting the annexed term (المضاف), meaning its lamp (مصباحها), and with the fatha on the ta' and the dal and the tashdid on the qaf (يُوَقَّدُ). مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ زَيْتُونَةٍ (from a blessed tree, an olive tree,) The piercing light of the lamp began from the olive tree (شجرة الزّيتون), whose benefit is abundant, in that its wick (زبالته) is saturated with its oil. لا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلا غَرْبِيَّةٍ (neither eastern nor western,) (Meaning) the sun does not fall upon it at one time and not another, but rather in a way that the sun falls upon it throughout the entire day, for its fruit is then riper and its oil purer. يَكادُ زَيْتُها يُضِيءُ وَلَوْ لَمْ تَمْسَسْهُ نارٌ (whose oil is almost luminous, though fire should not touch it.) That is, it is almost luminous by itself, without fire, due to its brilliance and excessive gleam. نُورٌ عَلى نُورٍ (Light upon light.) Multiplied light, for the light of the lamp is increased in its illumination by the purity of the oil, the clarity (زهرة) of the lantern (القنديل), and the containment (ضبط) of the niche (المشكاة) for its rays. يَهْدِي اللهُ لِنُورِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ (Allah guides to His Light whomever He wishes.) Meaning, to this piercing light. وَيَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْأَمْثالَ لِلنَّاسِ (And Allah draws parables for mankind,) Bringing the intelligible (المعقول) closer to the sensible (المحسوس) for clarification and explanation. وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (and Allah is all-aware of all things.) Whether intelligible (معقولاً) or sensible (محسوساً).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It is a parable that Allah the Almighty struck for us." And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding اللهُ نُورُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth), he said: "Thus is Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." Regarding مَثَلُ نُورِهِ (The parable of His Light), he said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." Regarding كَمِشْكاةٍ (is that of a niche), he said: "The chest (صدر) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." Regarding فِيها مِصْباحٌ (wherein there is a lamp), he said: "In it is the light of knowledge (نور العلم), meaning Prophethood (النبوّة)." Regarding الْمِصْباحُ فِي زُجاجَةٍ (The lamp is in a glass), he said: "The knowledge of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله transferred (صدر) to the heart of Ali (peace be upon him)." Regarding الزُّجاجَةُ كَأَنَّها (The glass is as if it were), he said: "As if it were." Regarding كَوْكَبٌ دُرِّيٌّ يُوقَدُ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ زَيْتُونَةٍ لا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلا غَرْبِيَّةٍ (a brilliant star lit from a blessed tree, an olive tree, neither eastern nor western), he said: "That is the Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), neither a Jew nor a Christian." Regarding يَكادُ زَيْتُها يُضِيءُ وَلَوْ لَمْ تَمْسَسْهُ نارٌ (whose oil is almost luminous, though fire should not touch it), he said: "Knowledge is almost uttered from the mouth of the scholar (العالم) from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) before he even speaks it." Regarding نُورٌ عَلى نُورٍ (Light upon light), he said: "The Imam following the Imam." And there are other narrations with this meaning.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith, He says: "I am the Guide (هادي) of the heavens and the earth. The parable of the knowledge I was given, which is My Light by which guidance is attained, is like the niche (المشكاة)." فِيها مِصْباحٌ (wherein there is a lamp). "The niche (المشكاة) is the heart of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, and the lamp (المصباح) is His Light which contains the knowledge." Regarding His saying: الْمِصْباحُ فِي زُجاجَةٍ (The lamp is in a glass), He says: "I desire to take you (O Muhammad), so place what you have with the Successor (الوصي), just as the lamp is placed الْمِصْباحُ فِي زُجاجَةٍ الزُّجاجَةُ كَأَنَّها كَوْكَبٌ دُرِّيٌّ (The lamp is in a glass. The glass is as if it were a brilliant star)." Thus, He informed them of the merit of the Successor (الوصيّ). يُوقَدُ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ (lit from a blessed tree). "The root of the blessed tree (الشجرة المباركة) is Abraham (peace be upon him), and this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: رَحْمَتُ اللهِ وَبَرَكاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ إِنَّهُ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ (Allah’s mercy and His blessings be upon you, members of the household. Indeed, He is all-praiseworthy, all-glorious.)
