And it is the saying of Allah: إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً وَآلَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَآلَ عِمْرانَ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ذُرِّيَّةً بَعْضُها مِنْ بَعْضٍ وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (Indeed, Allah chose Adam and Noah, and the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations—a progeny, some of them derived from the others; and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing.) لا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلا غَرْبِيَّةٍ (neither eastern nor western). He says: 'You are neither Jews, so that you pray before sunset, nor Christians, so that you pray before sunrise, but you are upon the religion (ملّة) of Abraham (peace be upon him).' And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said: ما كانَ إِبْراهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلا نَصْرانِيًّا وَلكِنْ كانَ حَنِيفاً مُسْلِماً وَما كانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ (Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian; rather he was a ḥanīf, a Muslim, and he was not one of the polytheists.) Regarding His saying: يَكادُ زَيْتُها يُضِيءُ (whose oil is almost luminous), He says: 'The parable of your children who are born from you is like the oil that is pressed from the olive; they are almost speaking with Prophethood (النبوّة) even if no angel descends upon them.'"

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both), regarding this Ayah: اللهُ نُورُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth), he said: "He began with the light of Himself." مَثَلُ نُورِهِ (The parable of His Light) is like His guidance (هداه) in the heart of the believer. كَمِشْكاةٍ فِيها مِصْباحٌ (is that of a niche wherein there is a lamp). "The niche (المشكاة) is the inner being (جوف) of the believer, the lantern (القنديل) is his heart, and the lamp (المصباح) is the Light which Allah placed within him." يُوقَدُ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ مُبارَكَةٍ (lit from a blessed tree). He said: "The tree (الشجرة) is the believer." زَيْتُونَةٍ لا شَرْقِيَّةٍ وَلا غَرْبِيَّةٍ (an olive tree, neither eastern nor western). He said: "It is on the level ground (سواء) of the mountain, neither western (لا غربيّة), meaning it has no east, nor eastern (لا شرقيّة), meaning it has no west. When the sun rises, it rises upon it, and when it sets, it sets upon it." يَكادُ زَيْتُها (whose oil is almost luminous), meaning the Light which Allah placed in his heart يُضِيءُ (is luminous) even if he does not speak. نُورٌ عَلى نُورٍ (Light upon light) is an obligation (فريضة) upon an obligation and a tradition (سنّة) upon a tradition. يَهْدِي اللهُ لِنُورِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ (Allah guides to His Light whomever He wishes). He said: "Allah guides to His obligations (فرائضه) and His traditions (سننه) whomever He wishes." وَيَضْرِبُ اللهُ الْأَمْثالَ لِلنَّاسِ (And Allah draws parables for mankind). He said: "This is a parable that Allah struck for the believer." He said: "The believer moves (يتقلّب) in five lights: his entrance is light, his exit is light, his knowledge is light, his speech is light, and his destination on the Day of Resurrection to Paradise is light." The narrator said: "I said to Ja'far (peace be upon him): 'They say (this parable is) the parable of the Lord's Light (نور الرّب).'" He said: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! There is no parable for Allah. Did He not say: فَلا تَضْرِبُوا لِلَّهِ الْأَمْثالَ (So do not strike similitudes for Allah)?"

(٣٦) فِي بُيُوتٍ ** أي (36) (In houses) meaning: Like a niche (كمشكاة) in some houses, or [the lamp] is kindled in houses, أَذِنَ اللهُ أَنْ تُرْفَعَ ** (which Allah has allowed to be raised) by magnification (بالتّعظيم), وَيُذْكَرَ فِيهَا اسْمُهُ (and His Name to be mentioned therein).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the houses of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And therein [in al-Kafi], and in al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the houses (بيوتات) of the Prophets, the Messengers, the wise men (الحكماء), and the Imams of guidance (أئمّة الهدى).

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): They are the houses of the Prophets, and the house of Ali (peace be upon him) is one of them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Qatadah said to him: "By Allah, I have sat before jurists (فقهاء) and in front of them, but my heart was never agitated before any one of them as it is agitated before you." He (peace be upon him) said to him: "Do you know where you are? You are before بُيُوتٍ أَذِنَ اللهُ أَنْ تُرْفَعَ (houses which Allah has allowed to be raised) until the end of the Ayah. You are there (ثمّة), and we are those [houses]." Qatadah then said to him: "You have spoken the truth, by Allah. May Allah make me your ransom (جعلني الله فداك). By Allah, they are not houses of stone (حجارة) or clay (طين)."

يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ فِيها بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصالِ ** (men glorify Him in them morning and evening) And it was recited with the fatḥah on the bā' (باء).

(٣٧) رِجالٌ لا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجارَةٌ وَلا بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ وَإِقامِ الصَّلاةِ وَإِيتاءِ الزَّكاةِ. (37) men whom neither trade nor bargaining distracts from the remembrance of Allah and the maintenance of prayer and the giving of zakat.

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "They were men of trade, but when the prayer was due, they abandoned trade and went forth to the prayer. And they have a greater reward (أجراً) than those who do not trade."

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): the like of it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated in a raised chain (رفعه), he said: "They are the merchants (التجّار) whom لا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجارَةٌ وَلا بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (neither trade nor bargaining distracts from the remembrance of Allah). When the times (مواقيت) for prayer arrived, they rendered to Allah His due right therein."

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a merchant (تاجر)—what had he done? It was said: "He is righteous (صالح), but he has abandoned trade." He (peace be upon him) said: "The work of Satan (عمل الشيطان)," three times. "Did he not know that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم bought a caravan (عير) that came from Syria (الشام), and profited therein enough to pay off His debt and distribute among His relatives?"

Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: رِجالٌ لا تُلْهِيهِمْ تِجارَةٌ وَلا بَيْعٌ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ (men whom neither trade nor bargaining distracts from the remembrance of Allah)—the Ayah. The storytellers (القصّاص) say that the people did not trade—they lied. Rather, they did not abandon the prayer at its appointed time (ميقاتها), and this is superior to one who attends the prayer but does not trade.

يَخافُونَ يَوْماً ** (They fear a day) despite the gratitude (الشكر) and obedience (الطاعة) they possess, تَتَقَلَّبُ فِيهِ الْقُلُوبُ وَالْأَبْصارُ ** (when the hearts and the sights will be overturned) and change due to the terror (الهول).

(٣٨) لِيَجْزِيَهُمُ اللهُ أَحْسَنَ ما عَمِلُوا وَيَزِيدَهُمْ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ ** (38) that Allah may reward them for the best of what they did, and enhance them out of His grace. things He did not promise them for their deeds, nor do they cross their minds. وَاللهُ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ ** And Allah provides for whomever He wishes without reckoning. An affirmation of the increase (الزيادة), and a drawing of attention to the perfection of power (القدرة), the execution of the Will (المشيئة), and the vastness of benevolence (الإحسان).

(٣٩) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَعْمالُهُمْ كَسَرابٍ بِقِيعَةٍ ** (39) As for the faithless, their deeds are like a mirage in a plain, (in) a level land (بِقِيعَةٍ). يَحْسَبُهُ الظَّمْآنُ ماءً which the thirsty man supposes to be water. حَتَّى إِذا جاءَهُ لَمْ يَجِدْهُ شَيْئاً ** When he comes to it, he finds it to be nothing, of what he supposed it to be, and he finds Allah with him, calling him to account. فَوَفَّاهُ حِسابَهُ وَاللهُ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ ** and He pays him his full account, and Allah is swift at reckoning. The reckoning of one does not distract Him from the reckoning of another, and its meaning has preceded. It is narrated: that it was revealed concerning Utbah ibn Rabi'ah ibn Umayyah, who engaged in worship (تَعَبَّدَ) during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) and sought religion (الدين). But when Islam came, he disbelieved (كَفَرَ).

(40) أَوْ كَظُلُماتٍ ** is conjoined (عطف) to كَسَرَابٍ (like a mirage), and أو (or) is for demarcation (التحييز). For their deeds, because they are vain (لاغية), have no benefit, like the mirage, and because they are devoid of the light of Truth (نور الحق), they are like the darknesses accumulated from the deep (لجّ) of the sea, the waves, and the clouds. Or (أو) is for diversification (التّنويع): for their deeds, if they were good, are like the mirage, and if they were ugly (قبيحة), they are like the darknesses.

فِي بَحْرٍ لُجِّيٍ ** (in a deep sea), deep, attributed to the depths (اللّجج), which is the main body of water. يَغْشاهُ ** (covers it), covers the sea, مَوْجٌ مِنْ فَوْقِهِ مَوْجٌ ** (a wave covers it, above which is a wave), meaning successive, accumulated waves. مِنْ فَوْقِهِ ** (above it), meaning from above the second wave, سَحابٌ ** (a cloud), which covered the stars and veiled their lights. ظُلُماتٌ ** (darknesses). These are darknesses, and it was recited with kasra (بالجرّ) based on it being a substitute (إبدال) for the first (darknesses) or by adding سحاب (cloud) to it. بَعْضُها فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ إِذا أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُ ** (some of them above others. If he brings out his hand), meaning the one who is there, لَمْ يَكَدْ يَراها ** (he would scarcely see it), he would not come close to seeing it, let alone showing it. وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللهُ لَهُ نُوراً ** (And for him whom Allah does not appoint a light), and whoever Allah has not decreed guidance for Him, and has not granted him success (يوفّقه) in its means, فَما لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ ** (he has no light), unlike the one granted success (الموفّق) who has light upon light.

أَوْ كَظُلُماتٍ فِي بَحْرٍ لُجِّيٍّ يَغْشاهُ مَوْجٌ مِنْ فَوْقِهِ مَوْجٌ مِنْ فَوْقِهِ سَحابٌ ظُلُماتٌ بَعْضُها فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ إِذا أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُ لَمْ يَكَدْ يَراها وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللهُ لَهُ نُوراً فَما لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ (٤٠) (Or like the darknesses in a deep sea, covered by a wave, above which is a wave, above which is a cloud, darknesses piled one upon another. When he brings out his hand, he would scarcely see it. And for him whom Allah does not appoint a light, he has no light.)

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): أَوْ كَظُلُماتٍ ** He said: The first (الأوّل) and his companion (صاحبه). يَغْشاهُ مَوْجٌ ** The third (الثالث). مِنْ فَوْقِهِ مَوْجٌ ** The darknesses of the second (الثاني). بَعْضُها فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ ** Mu'awiyah (may Allah curse him) and the tribulations (فتن) of Banu Umayyah. إِذا أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُ ** The believer in the darkness of their tribulation. لَمْ يَكَدْ يَراها وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللهُ لَهُ نُوراً ** An Imam from the progeny of Fatimah (peace be upon her). **فَما لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ ** An Imam on the Day of Resurrection.

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): أَوْ كَظُلُماتٍ ** So-and-so and so-and-so (فلان وفلان). فِي بَحْرٍ لُجِّيٍّ يَغْشاهُ مَوْجٌ ** Meaning Na'thal (نعثل). مِنْ فَوْقِهِ مَوْجٌ ** Talhah and Al-Zubayr. بَعْضُها فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ ** Mu'awiyah and Yazid (may Allah curse them) and the tribulations (فتن) of Banu Umayyah. إِذا أَخْرَجَ يَدَهُ ** (If he brings out his hand) in the darkness of their tribulation لَمْ يَكَدْ يَراها ** (he would scarcely see it). وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ اللهُ لَهُ نُوراً ** (And for him whom Allah does not appoint a light), meaning an Imam from the progeny of Fatimah (peace be upon her), فَما لَهُ مِنْ نُورٍ ** (he has no light), so he has no Imam whose light he walks by, just as in His Almighty saying: يَسْعى نُورُهُمْ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ ** (Their light runs before them and on their right). He said: Indeed, the believers on the Day of Resurrection, نُورُهُمْ يَسْعى بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ ** (Their light runs before them and on their right), until they descend to their stations in the Gardens (الجنان).

(٤١) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالطَّيْرُ ** (41) Do you not see that Allah is glorified by everyone in the heavens and the earth, and the birds also, صَافَّاتٍ (in rows), standing in the atmosphere, with wings lined up in the air كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلاتَهُ وَتَسْبِيحَهُ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِما يَفْعَلُونَ ** (41) in array? Each knows its prayer and glorification, and Allah knows well what they do.

Some of the people of spiritual knowledge (أهل المعرفة) said: Allah created creation so that they might glorify Him. Thus, their utterance is glorification (التسبيح) of Him, praise (الثناء) of Him, and prostration (السجود) to Him. So He said: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالطَّيْرُ ** (the Ayah). And He also said: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يَسْجُدُ لَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ وَالنُّجُومُ (the Ayah). And by these two verses, He addressed His Prophet, whom He made witness that and showed it to him, saying: أَلَمْ تَرَ ** (Do you not see?), and He did not say: أَلَمْ تَرَوْا (Do you not see [plural]?). For what we have not seen is faith (ايمان) for us, but for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم it is direct sight (عيان). Thus, He made him witness the prostration (سجود) and humility (تواضعه) of everything to Allah. And everyone whom Allah makes witness that and sees it falls under this address.

And this is an innate glorification (تسبيح فطري) and an essential prostration (سجود ذاتي) that arises from a manifestation (تجل) which manifested to them, so they loved Him, and thus they were prompted to praise Him, not by obligation (تكليف) but by essential necessity (اقتضاء ذاتي). And this is the essential worship (العبادة الذاتية) in which Allah established them by virtue of the deservingness (الاستحقاق) that He deserves. He said: And this glorification (التسبيح) is not by the tongue of condition (لسان الحال), as is claimed by the people of theoretical speculation (أهل النظر) who lack spiritual unveiling (كشف). He said: And we, along with faith in the reports (الأخبار), have been granted the spiritual unveiling (الكشف). We have indeed heard the stones remembering Allah, a direct sight (رؤية عين), with a tongue that our ears hear from them, and they address us with the address of those who know the lawful (حلال) of Allah, something that not every human being perceives.

I say: Further clarification of this has preceded in Surat al-Nahl and Surat Bani Isra'il.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): No bird is hunted on land or sea, nor is any wild animal hunted, except due to its neglecting glorification (التسبيح).

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Verily, Allah has an angel in the form of a speckled, ash-colored rooster (الديك الأملح الأشهب). Its talons are in the seventh earth, and its comb is beneath the Throne (العرش). It has two wings: one wing in the East and one wing in the West. As for the wing in the East, it is made of snow (ثلج), and as for the wing in the West, it is made of fire (نار). Whenever the time for prayer approaches, it stands upon its talons and raises its comb beneath the Throne. Then it inclines one of its wings toward the other, clapping them together just as the rooster claps in your dwellings. Neither does the one made of snow extinguish the fire, nor does the one made of fire melt the snow. Then it calls out with its loudest voice: "I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and Messenger, the Seal of the Prophets (خاتم النبيين), and that His successor (وصيّه) is the best of successors (خير الوصيّين). Glorified (سبّوح), Holy (قدّوس), Lord of the angels and the Spirit (الروح)." There remains no rooster on earth except that it responds to it. And that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَالطَّيْرُ صَافَّاتٍ كُلٌّ قَدْ عَلِمَ صَلاتَهُ وَتَسْبِيحَهُ.

And in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A similar narration.

(٤٢) وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَإِلَى اللهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** (42) To Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and toward Allah is the ultimate destination. The return/destination of all. (٤٣) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللهَ يُزْجِي ** Have you not regarded that Allah drives the clouds, then He joins them together, then He piles them up, whereat you see the rain issuing from their midst? And He sends down from the sky mountains containing hail, and strikes with it whomever He wishes, and turns it away from whomever He wishes. The flash of His lightning almost takes away the sight. drives سَحاباً ثُمَّ يُؤَلِّفُ بَيْنَهُ ** by being pieces, so He joins some of it to some [other part] ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُ رُكاماً piled up, some of it upon some [other part] فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ the rain يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلالِهِ ** from its fissures (فُتُوقِهِ), the plural of خلل وَيُنَزِّلُ مِنَ السَّماءِ ** from the clouds (الْغَمَامِ), for everything that is above you is سماء (sky/heaven). مِنْ جِبالٍ ** from massive pieces resembling mountains in their size and solidity (جُمُودِهَا) فِيها مِنْ بَرَدٍ ** an explanation/clarification for the mountains (الْجِبَالِ) فَيُصِيبُ بِهِ ** with it, meaning the hail (الْبَرَدِ) مَنْ يَشاءُ وَيَصْرِفُهُ عَنْ مَنْ يَشاءُ. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made the clouds sieves (غَرَابِيل) for the rain. They melt the hail (الْبَرَد) into water so that it does not harm anything it strikes. And that which you see in it of hail (الْبَرَد) and thunderbolts (الصَّوَاعِق) is a retribution (نِقْمَة) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, with which He strikes whomever He wills among His servants." And in it (al-Kafi), narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Hail (الْبَرَد) is not eaten because Allah the Almighty strikes with it whomever He wills." And in al-Ihlilijah, narrated from him (peace be upon him), in a tradition mentioning the winds (الرِّيَاح), he said: "And by them (بِهَا) the separated parts are joined together, and by the two of them (بِهِمَا) the covering clouds (الْغَمَام الْمُطَبَّق) are separated until they spread out in the sky however He wills, being managed (مُدَبَّرَة). So يَجْعَلُهُ كِسَفاً فَتَرَى الْوَدْقَ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ خِلالِهِ ** with a known measure for an understood livelihood (مَعَاش مَفْهُوم), apportioned provisions (أَرْزَاق مَقْسُومَة), and written terms (آجَال مَكْتُوبَة)." And in al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), in a tradition mentioning the types of winds (الرِّيَاح), he said: "And among them are winds that hold the clouds between the sky and the earth, and winds that squeeze the clouds, so they rain by the permission of Allah, and winds that disperse the clouds." يَكادُ سَنا بَرْقِهِ ** the light of His lightning يَذْهَبُ بِالْأَبْصارِ ** the sights of those looking at it, due to the excessiveness of the illumination (الْإِضَاءَة).

(٤٤) يُقَلِّبُ اللهُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ ** (Allah alternates the night and the day.) by alternating between them (بالمعاقبة), and the decrease of one of them and the increase of the other, and the changing of their states with heat and cold and darkness and light. إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ ** Indeed, in that in what has been mentioned previously لَعِبْرَةً لِأُولِي الْأَبْصارِ. there is indeed a lesson for those who have insight.

(٤٥) وَاللهُ خَلَقَ كُلَّ دَابَّةٍ ** (45) And Allah created every creature that crawls Every animal that crawls upon the earth. And it was read (خالق) (Creator) with the idafa (genitive construction) مِنْ ماءٍ (from water). Al-Qummi (narrated) that it is from semen (منّي). And it was said: from the water which is part of its substance, since among animals are those that are generated not from sperm (النطفة). فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَمْشِي عَلى بَطْنِهِ ** (so among them are those that walk on their bellies,) like the snake (كالحيّة). وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَمْشِي عَلى رِجْلَيْنِ ** (and among them are those that walk on two feet,) like humans (كالانس) and birds (والطّير). وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَمْشِي عَلى أَرْبَعٍ (and among them are those that walk on four) like livestock (كالنّعم) and wild beasts (والوحش). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام. And al-Qummi (narrated) from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: And among them are those that walk on more than that. يَخْلُقُ اللهُ ما يَشاءُ ** (Allah creates whatever He wishes.) from what has been mentioned and what has not been mentioned, according to the requirement of His Will (مشيّته). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (Indeed Allah has power over all things.)

(٤٦) لَقَدْ أَنْزَلْنا آياتٍ مُبَيِّناتٍ ** (46) We have certainly sent down manifest signs. [manifest signs] for the realities/truths by means of various kinds of proofs. وَاللهُ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ ** and Allah guides whomever He wishes by granting success (التوفيق) for reflecting upon them and contemplating their meanings إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ to a straight path, which leads to attaining the Truth and achieving triumph with Paradise (الْجَنَّةِ).

(٤٧) وَيَقُولُونَ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَبِالرَّسُولِ وَأَطَعْنا ** (47) And they say, 'We believe in Allah and the Apostle, and we obey.' to the two of them ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّى فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ ** by refraining from accepting His ruling مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ after this saying of theirs وَما أُولئِكَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ whom you know, and they are those who are sincere (المخلصون) in faith, firm (الثابتون) upon it.

(٤٨) وَإِذا دُعُوا إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ ** (48) And when they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle, that He may judge between them Meaning, that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله may judge, for he is the judge outwardly (ظاهراً) and the one summoned to. And the mention of Allah is for His glorification (لتعظيمه) and to indicate that his judgment is, in reality, the judgment of Allah. إِذا فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ مُعْرِضُونَ A group among them suddenly exhibits aversion (الاعراض) when the truth is against them, due to their knowledge that He will not judge in their favor. This is an explanation of the turning away (التولّي) and an exaggeration thereof.

(٤٩) وَإِنْ يَكُنْ لَهُمُ الْحَقُ ** (49) And if the right be with them, not against them يَأْتُوا إِلَيْهِ مُذْعِنِينَ ** they come to Him submissively. submitting (مُنْقَادِينَ) because of their knowledge that He will judge in their favor. (٥٠) أَفِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ ** (50) Is there a sickness in their hearts, disbelief (كُفْر) and inclination towards injustice أَمِ ارْتابُوا ** or are they doubtful, in that they saw an accusation (تُهْمَة) from you, so their trust in you vanished أَمْ يَخافُونَ أَنْ يَحِيفَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَرَسُولُهُ ** or do they fear that Allah and His Apostle will be unjust to them in the judgment? بَلْ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ (Nay, it is they who are the wrongdoers.) This is a retraction (إِضْرَاب) from the last two divisions (doubt and fear of injustice) in order to confirm the first division (sickness in the heart), and the separation (الْفَصْل) is to negate that (injustice) from others, especially the one invited to His judgment.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and Uthman. That was because there was a dispute between them over a garden. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "We are content with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." Then Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf said to Uthman: "Do not seek judgment from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, for He will judge in his favor against you. Rather, seek judgment from Ibn Shaybah the Jew." Uthman then said to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "We are not content except with Ibn Shaybah the Jew." Ibn Shaybah then said to Uthman: "Do you trust the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله with the revelation of the heavens, yet you accuse Him concerning judgments?" So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to His Messenger: **وَإِذا دُعُوا إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ** (And when they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle) (the verses).

And in al-Majma': Al-Balkhi narrated that there was a dispute between Ali and Uthman concerning a piece of land that Uthman had bought from Ali. Stones appeared in it, and Uthman wanted to return it due to a defect (بِالْعَيْب), but Ali did not accept it. So Uthman said: "Between me and you is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." Then al-Hakam ibn Abi al-As said: "If you seek judgment from his cousin (ابْنِ عَمِّهِ), He will judge in his favor, so do not seek judgment from Him." Thus, the verses were revealed. He said: This is what is narrated from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him), or something close to it.

(٥١) إِنَّما كانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (The believers’ only saying is) في المجمع عن عليّ عليه السلام : انّه قرء قول المؤمنين بالرّفع In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He read (قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ) with the raf' (nominative case). إِذا دُعُوا إِلَى اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَنْ يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ (when they are summoned to Allah and His Apostle that He may judge between them, they say, ‘We hear and obey.’ And it is they who are the felicitous.) في المجمع عن الباقر عليه السلام والقمّيّ : انّ المعنيّ بالآية أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi: The one meant by the Ayah is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٥٢) وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَيَخْشَ اللهَ وَيَتَّقْهِ ** (52) And whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle, and fears Allah and is wary of Him And it was recited without al-ishba' (الاشباع), and with sukun (سكون) on the hā' (الهاء), and with sukun (سكون) on the qāf (القاف). فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفائِزُونَ ** it is they who are the felicitous. with the everlasting bliss (النعيم المقيم). (٥٣) وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ لَئِنْ أَمَرْتَهُمْ ** (53) They swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths that if you command them to leave their homes and their wealth, they would surely leave. لَيَخْرُجُنَّ قُلْ لا تُقْسِمُوا على الكذب طاعَةٌ مَعْرُوفَةٌ they will surely go forth. Say, 'Do not swear. Obedience is known. What is required of you is a known obedience (طاعة معروفة), not the oath (اليمين) upon the reprehensible hypocritical (النّفاقيّة) obedience. إِنَّ اللهَ خَبِيرٌ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ ** Indeed Allah is well aware of what you do.' so your secrets (سرائركم) are not hidden from Him.

(54) قُلْ أَطِيعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ** (Say, 'Obey Allah and obey the Apostle.') [It is] an order to convey what Allah addressed them with, using the narrative form (على الحكاية), as an exaggeration in rebuking them. فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّما عَلَيْهِ ** (But if you turn away, then he is only responsible for what he is charged with) [The pronoun عليه refers] to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, [meaning] what He was charged with concerning the conveyance (التبليغ). وَعَلَيْكُمْ ما حُمِّلْتُمْ ** (and you are responsible for what you are charged with.) [Meaning] concerning compliance (الامتثال). وَإِنْ تُطِيعُوهُ تَهْتَدُوا ** (If you obey him, you will be guided.) To the truth (الحقّ). وَما عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلاغُ الْمُبِينُ ** (The Apostle is responsible only for a manifest conveyance.) The clear conveyance (التبليغ الواضح) of what you were obligated with (كلّفتم). And he has fulfilled [his duty]. What remains is only what you were charged with. If you fulfill it, it is for your benefit, and if you turn away, it is against you.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), in a sermon describing the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "And he fulfilled what He was charged with concerning the burdens (أثقال) of prophethood."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O assembly of the reciters of the Quran, fear Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, regarding what He has charged you with from His Book. For I am responsible, and you are responsible. I am responsible for conveying the message (تبليغ الرسالة), and as for you, you will be questioned concerning what you were charged with from the Book of Allah and my Sunnah (سُنَّتي)."

** (55) وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Allah has promised those of you who have faith and do righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors in the land) meaning, He will surely make them successors (خلفاء) after your Prophet, كَمَا اسْتَخْلَفَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (just as He made those who were before them successors). meaning, the successors (أوصياء) of the Prophets after them. And it was recited (وقرء) with a ḍammah on the tā’ and a kasrah on the lām (لَيُسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ). وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ دِينَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضى لَهُمْ (and that He will surely establish for them their religion which He has approved for them), which is Islam, وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِمْ (and that He will surely change their state after their fear) of the enemies—and it was recited (وقرء) with a lightened form (بالتخفيف)—أَمْناً (to security) from them. يَعْبُدُونَنِي لا يُشْرِكُونَ بِي شَيْئاً وَمَنْ كَفَرَ ** (They shall worship Me, not associating anything with Me. And whoever is ungrateful) meaning, apostatized (ارتد) or was ungrateful for this blessing (النعمة) بَعْدَ ذلِكَ (after that) after its attainment فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ ** (it is they who are the transgressors). meaning, those who are complete in their transgression (فسقهم) since they apostatized after the clarity of the matter, or were ungrateful for that great blessing.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "They are the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them)."

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "And Allah has surely said in His Book, specifically to the guardians of authority (ولاة الأمر) after Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله: وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ ** up to His saying وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ. He says: 'I have made you successors for My knowledge, My religion, and My worship after your Prophet, just as I made the successors (أوصياء) of Adam (peace be upon him) successors after him, until the Prophet who follows him is sent.' يَعْبُدُونَنِي لا يُشْرِكُونَ بِي شَيْئاً ** He says: يَعْبُدُونَنِي ** (They shall worship Me) with faith (بإِيمان). There is no Prophet after Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. So whoever says otherwise, فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ . The guardians of authority (ولاة الأمر) after Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله have been established with knowledge, and We are them, so ask Us. If We speak truthfully to you, then acknowledge it. But you will not do so."

And al-Qummi (said): It was revealed concerning the Qā’im (القائم) from the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon them).

I say: The changing of their fear to security (الأمن) occurs through the Qā’im (القائم), or the totality of that (promise) together occurs through Him. Thus, it does not contradict the previous narration.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Household of the Prophet (peace be upon them): It is concerning the Mahdi (المهديّ) from the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon them).

He said: And al-Ayyashi narrated with his chain of transmission from Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both): He recited the Ayah and said: "By Allah, they are our followers (شيعتنا), the Household of the Prophet (أهل البيت). This will be done for them at the hands of a man from Us, and He is the Mahdi (مهديّ) of this Ummah. He is the one about whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: 'If there remained of the world but one day, Allah would surely prolong that day until a man from My progeny (عترتي), whose name is My name, takes charge. He will fill the earth with justice and equity as it was filled with oppression and tyranny.'"

He said: And the like of this was narrated from Abu Ja‘far and Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon them both). He said: Based on this, the intended meaning of الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ is the Prophet and His Household (أهل بيته).

I say: So His saying (peace be upon him): هم والله شيعتنا يفعل ذلك بهم (By Allah, they are our followers, this will be done for them) means that the changing of fear to security (تبديل الخوف بالأمن) will only be for them.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), concerning the story of Noah and the mention of the believers among his people awaiting relief (الفرج) until Allah showed them the succession (الاستخلاف) and establishment (التمكين). He said: "Likewise is the Qā’im (peace be upon him). His period of occultation (غيبته) will be prolonged so that the pure truth (الحق عن محضه) may be clarified, and faith (الإِيمان) may be purified from impurity by the apostasy (ارتداد) of every person whose nature (طينته) was corrupt (خبيثة) among the followers (الشيعة) who are feared to harbor hypocrisy (النفاق) when they sense the succession (الاستخلاف), the establishment (التمكين), and the widespread authority (الامر المنتشر) during the era of the Qā’im (peace be upon him)." The narrator said: I said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, these Nawāṣib (النواصب) claim that this Ayah was revealed concerning Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali." He said: "No, Allah does not guide the hearts of the Nāṣibah (الناصبة). When was the religion that Allah and His Messenger approved established with the widespread authority (انتشار الامر) among the Ummah, the departure of fear from their hearts, and the removal of doubt from their breasts during the era of any one of these? And during the era of Ali (peace be upon him), there was the apostasy of the Muslims, the tribulations (الفتن) that used to flare up in their days, and the wars that used to break out between the disbelievers and them."

In al-Iḥtijāj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a narration where He mentioned the faults (مثالب) of the three (Caliphs) and Allah's respite (امهال) to them. He said: "All of that is so that the respite (النظرة) which Allah obligated for His enemy, Iblis, may be completed, until the Book reaches its term (أجله), and the truth is established against the disbelievers, and the true promise (الوعد الحقّ) which Allah clarified in His Book by His saying draws near: وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ كَمَا اسْتَخْلَفَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Allah has promised those of you who have faith and do righteous deeds that He will surely make them successors in the land, just as He made those who were before them successors). And that is when nothing remains of Islam except its name, and nothing remains of the Quran except its script (رسمه). And the Master of the Affair (صاحب الامر) will be absent, with the excuse for Him being clarified due to the tribulation (الفتنة) encompassing the hearts, until the person closest to Him is the most severe in enmity towards Him. And at that time, Allah will support Him with armies you have not seen, and He will make the religion of His Prophet manifest through His hands, and لِيُظْهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ.** (that He may make it prevail over all religions, though the polytheists should be averse.)"

In al-Jawāmi', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He said: "The earth was gathered for me, so I saw its eastern and western parts, and the dominion of My Ummah will reach what was gathered for Me from it."

And al-Miqdad narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله that He said: "No house of clay (مدر) nor house of hair (وبر) will remain on earth except that Allah will cause the word of Islam (كلمة الإِسلام) to enter it, by the glory of the glorious (عزّ عزيز) or the humiliation of the humiliated (ذلّ ذليل). Either Allah will grant them glory and make them among its people, or He will humiliate them and they will submit to it."

(56) وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (56) And maintain the prayer and give the zakat and obey the Apostle, so that you may be shown mercy. (57) لا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (frustrating Allah from reaching and destroying them). And it was read with the yā’ (وقرئ بالياء). وَ مَأْواهُمُ النَّارُ وَ لَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ. (57) Do not suppose that the faithless can frustrate [Him] in the land. And their refuge is the Fire, and what a wretched destination! (58) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِيَسْتَأْذِنْكُمُ الَّذِينَ مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُكُمْ In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "This is specific to men, not women." He was asked: "Do women then seek permission during these three hours?" He said: "No, rather they enter and exit." In another narration: "They are the male and female slaves, and the children who have not reached puberty." وَالَّذِينَ لَمْ يَبْلُغُوا الْحُلُمَ مِنْكُمْ ** (The children among the free people (الْأَحْرَار)). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Regarding مِنْ أَنْفُسِكُمْ (of yourselves), He said: "You must seek permission just as one who has reached puberty seeks permission during these three hours." ثَلاثَ مَرَّاتٍ ** (meaning, during the day and night): مِنْ قَبْلِ صَلاةِ الْفَجْرِ ** (because it is the time of rising from the beds, casting off sleeping clothes, and putting on waking clothes); وَحِينَ تَضَعُونَ ثِيابَكُمْ ** (meaning, for the midday nap (لِلْقَيْلُولَة)) مِنَ الظَّهِيرَةِ ** (which is an explanation for the time (لِلْحِين), meaning the time of al-Zuhr); وَمِنْ بَعْدِ صَلاةِ الْعِشاءِ (because it is the time of undressing and wrapping oneself in a blanket). ثَلاثُ عَوْراتٍ لَكُمْ ** (Meaning, three times for you during which your covering is disturbed (يَخْتَلّ فِيهَا تَسَتّرُكُمْ). The origin of العورة (al-ʿawrah) is defect (الخَلَل). And ثَلاث was read in the accusative case (بالنصب)). لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلا عَلَيْهِمْ جُناحٌ بَعْدَهُنَ ** (after these times, regarding the omission of seeking permission). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "And your slave and your young servants (غِلْمَانُكُمْ) may enter after these three times of privacy without permission, if they wish." طَوَّافُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (Meaning, they are frequent attendants (طَوَّافُونَ). This is a new sentence (اسْتِينَاف) to explain the excuse that permits the omission of seeking permission, which is mixing (الْمُخَالَطَة) and frequent entering (كَثْرَة الْمُدَاخَلَة)). بَعْضُكُمْ ** (attending) عَلى بَعْضٍ ** (These are for service, and those are for being served (لِلِاسْتِخْدَام), for if the servant is absent, there is a need to request [him], and likewise children are needed for upbringing (لِلتَّرْبِيَة)). كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الْآياتِ ** (meaning, the rulings (الْأَحْكَام)) وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ ** (of your circumstances (بِأَحْوَالِكُمْ)) حَكِيمٌ ** (in what He has legislated for you). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "Let those whom your right hands possess and those of you who have not reached puberty [seek permission] three times as Allah has commanded you." He said: "And whoever reaches puberty should not enter upon his mother, nor upon his sister, nor upon his maternal aunt, nor upon anyone besides that, except with permission. So do not grant permission until he gives the greeting (يُسَلِّم), for the greeting (السَّلَام) is obedience to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ)." And He said: "And let your servant seek permission from you when he reaches puberty during the three times of privacy (عَوْرَات) if he enters during any of them, even if his house is within your house." He said: "And let him seek permission from you after the al-ʿIshāʾ (night prayer), which is called al-ʿAtamah (الْعَتَمَة), and when you wake up in the morning (تُصْبِح), and when you put aside your clothes at noon." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), only commanded this for privacy (لِلْخَلْوَة), for it is a time of honor (عِزَّة) and privacy (خَلْوَة). And al-Qummi said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty forbade anyone from entering upon anyone else during these three times—neither a father, nor a sister, nor a mother, nor a servant—except with permission." (59) وَإِذا بَلَغَ الْأَطْفالُ مِنْكُمُ ** (O free people (أَيّهَا الْأَحْرَار)) الْحُلُمَ فَلْيَسْتَأْذِنُوا فِي جَمِيعِ الْأَوْقاتِ كَمَا اسْتَأْذَنَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Those who reached puberty before them among the free people (الْأَحْرَار) who seek permission at all times). And the free people (الْأَحْرَار) were addressed by this because the puberty of the free people necessitates the removal of the ruling mentioned regarding the specification of seeking permission to the three times, unlike the puberty of the slaves, for the ruling remains with them regarding the specification due to the need for service and being served (الِاسْتِخْدَام). And what indicates this from the text (النَّصّ) has already passed. كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمْ آياتِهِ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (He repeated it for emphasis and exaggeration in the command to seek permission). (59) But when the children among you reach puberty, let them ask permission at all times, just as those who were before them asked permission. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you, and Allah is All-knowing, All-wise.**

(٦٠) وَالْقَواعِدُ مِنَ النِّساءِ اللّاتي لا يَرْجُونَ نِكاحاً فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ جُناحٌ أَنْ يَضَعْنَ ثِيابَهُنَ ** (And the elderly women who are past childbearing, who do not expect to marry, there is no sin upon them if they put aside their clothes) The elderly women (العجائز) who have ceased (قعدن) from menstruation (الحيض) and marriage (النكاح), those who do not expect to marry (اللّاتي لا يَرْجُونَ نِكاحاً), meaning they do not hope for it due to their old age (لكبرهنّ). there is no sin upon them if they put aside their clothes meaning the outer garments (الثياب الظاهرة).

And in al-Majma': Al-Baqir and Al-Sadiq (peace be upon them) read it as: يضعن من ثيابهنّ (if they put aside some of their clothes).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the elderly women who have despaired of menstruation (المحيض) and marriage (التزويج), that they may put aside the clothes (الثياب).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited it and said: The جلباب (outer garment) and the خمار (head covering) if the woman is elderly (مسنّة).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: The خمار and the جلباب. It was asked: "In front of whom?" He said: "In front of whomever."

And in one narration, He said: She puts aside the جلباب alone. And in another: Except if she is a slave-woman (أمة), there is no sin upon her if she puts aside her خمار. This was narrated in al-Tahdhib.

And in al-Uyun, narrated from Al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: Other than the جلباب. He said: So there is no harm in looking at the hair (شعور) of women like them.

غَيْرَ مُتَبَرِّجاتٍ بِزِينَةٍ ** (without displaying adornment) Not displaying adornment (زينة) from what they were commanded to conceal in His Almighty saying: وَلا يُبْدِينَ زِينَتَهُنَّ إِلَّا ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها ** (and let them not display their adornment except what is manifest of it) As narrated in al-Kafi from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: And the adornment (الزّينة) which they may display for them is something mentioned in the other Ayah.

I say: And this is the face (الوجه), the two hands (الكفّان), and the two feet (القدمان), as previously mentioned. And whatever is besides that is included in the prohibition (النهي) against displaying it (التبرج بها)! And the origin of التبرّج (displaying adornment) is the effort (التكلّف) involved in revealing what is hidden.

وَأَنْ يَسْتَعْفِفْنَ خَيْرٌ لَهُنَ ** (But to abstain is better for them) Than putting aside the clothes (من الوضع).

Al-Qummi said: Meaning, they should not display themselves to men (للرّجال).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: If she does not do so, then it is better for her.

وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ ** (and Allah is All-hearing,) To their speech (لمقالهنّ) to men (للرّجال).

عَلِيمٌ ** (All-knowing.) Of their intentions (بمقصودهنّ).

(٦١) لَيْسَ عَلَى الْأَعْمى حَرَجٌ وَلا عَلَى الْأَعْرَجِ حَرَجٌ وَلا عَلَى الْمَرِيضِ حَرَجٌ ** (61) There is no blame on the blind, nor is there any blame on the lame, nor is there any blame on the sick, [This is] a negation of what they used to feel constraint about: eating with the healthy, fearing that the healthy might find them repulsive; or eating from the house of one who gives them the key and permits them freedom of access therein when he goes out to battle and leaves them in charge of the dwellings, fearing that this might not be done with a willing heart; or accepting the invitation of those who invite them to the houses of their fathers, their children, and their relatives, and feeding them, lest they become a burden (كَلًّا) upon them.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: That was because the people of Medina, before they embraced Islam, used to isolate the blind, the lame, and the sick, and they would not eat with them. The Ansar had a sense of pride (تِيه) and generosity (تَكْرِمَة). They said: "The blind man does not see the food, the lame man cannot endure the crowding around the food, and the sick person does not eat as the healthy person eats." So they isolated their food for them on the side, and they considered it a sin (جُنَاح) to eat with them. The blind, the lame, and the sick used to say: "Perhaps we harm them if we eat with them," so they refrained from eating with them. When the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) arrived, they asked him about that, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَأْكُلُوا جَمِيعاً أَوْ أَشْتاتاً (there is no blame on you if you eat together or separately.)

**وَلا عَلى أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَنْ تَأْكُلُوا مِنْ بُيُوتِكُمْ ** (nor on yourselves if you eat from your own houses,) It was said that this means the houses where your wives and dependents are, and this includes the houses of the children, because the child's house is like his own house, due to His saying (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "You and your wealth belong to your father," and His saying: "Indeed, the purest thing a man eats is from his earning, and his child is from his earning."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what is permissible for a man from his child's wealth. He said: "Sustenance (قُوت) without extravagance (سَرَف) if he is compelled to it." It was asked: "Then what about the saying of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to the man who brought his father forward: 'You and your wealth belong to your father'?" He said: "That man only came with his father to the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this is my father, and he has wronged me regarding my inheritance from my mother.' The father then informed him that he had spent it on him and on himself. So he [the Prophet] said: 'You and your wealth belong to your father.' And the man had nothing [left]. The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) would not detain the father for the son."

أَوْ بُيُوتِ آبائِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أُمَّهاتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ إِخْوانِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخَواتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَعْمامِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ عَمَّاتِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ أَخْوالِكُمْ أَوْ بُيُوتِ خالاتِكُمْ أَوْ ما مَلَكْتُمْ مَفاتِحَهُ. (or your fathers’ houses, or your mothers’ houses, or your brothers’ houses, or your sisters’ houses, or your paternal uncles’ houses, or your paternal aunts’ houses, or your maternal uncles’ houses, or your maternal aunts’ houses, or [the houses] whose keys you possess.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: [This refers to] the man who has an agent (وَكِيل) managing his property, and he [the agent] eats without his permission.

And narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): There is no sin (جُنَاح) upon you regarding what you feed [others] and what you eat from that whose keys you possess, as long as you do not spoil it.

أَوْ صَدِيقِكُمْ. (or your friends’ [houses].)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Imams of Guidance (أَئِمَّة الهُدَى) (peace be upon them), they said: There is no harm in these people eating from the houses of those whom Allah mentioned, according to their need, without extravagance (إِسْرَاف).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what He means by His saying: **أَوْ صَدِيقِكُمْ ** (or your friends’ [houses]). He said: "By Allah, it is the man who enters his friend's house and eats without his permission."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): These people whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named in this verse may eat without their permission from dates (التَّمْر) and seasoned food (المَأْدُوم). Likewise, a woman may feed [others] from her husband's house without his permission. But as for food other than that, no.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: It is permissible for the woman to eat and to give charity (تَصَدَّق), and for the friend to eat from his brother's house and give charity.

In al-Jawami', narrated from him (peace be upon him): Due to the greatness of the sanctity (حُرْمَة) of the friend, He made him, in terms of familiarity (الأُنْس), trust (الثِّقَة), ease (الِانْبِسَاط), and abandoning formality (تَرْك الحِشْمَة), equal in status to the soul, the father, the brother, and the son.

**لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَأْكُلُوا جَمِيعاً أَوْ أَشْتاتاً ** (there is no blame on you if you eat together or separately.) [Meaning] gathered together or dispersed.

Al-Qummi [narrated]: When the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) migrated to Medina and established brotherhood (آخَى) between the Muslims from the Muhajirun and the Ansar, he said: Thereafter, when he sent any of his companions on a campaign (غَزَاة) or a detachment (سَرِّيَّة), the man would hand the key of his house to his brother in faith and say to him: "Take whatever you wish and eat whatever you wish." But they would refrain from doing so, to the extent that the food in the house might sometimes spoil. So Allah revealed: لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَأْكُلُوا جَمِيعاً أَوْ أَشْتاتاً (there is no blame on you if you eat together or separately), meaning whether its owner is present or not, provided you possess its keys.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: **لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ ** (there is no blame on you), he said: "With permission or without permission."

فَإِذا دَخَلْتُمْ بُيُوتاً فَسَلِّمُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِكُمْ تَحِيَّةً مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ مُبارَكَةً طَيِّبَةً. (So when you enter houses, greet yourselves with a greeting from Allah, blessed and good.)

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: It is the man's greeting upon the people of the house when he enters, and then they return the greeting to him, so it is your greeting upon yourselves.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), similar to this. And al-Qummi said: It is your greeting upon the people of the house, and their returning it to you, so it is your greeting upon yourself.

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: When a man among you enters his house, if there is someone in it, he should greet them. And if there is no one in it, he should say: "Peace be upon us from our Lord." Allah says: تَحِيَّةً مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ مُبارَكَةً طَيِّبَةً. (a greeting from Allah, blessed and good.)

In al-Jawami', He described it as blessed (بَرَكَة) and good (طَيِّب) because it is the invocation (دَعْوَة) of a believer for a believer, hoping thereby for an increase in goodness and purity of provision (طِيب الرِّزْق) from Allah.

And from this is His saying (peace be upon him): "Greet the people of your house, and the goodness of your house will increase."

كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الْآياتِ ** (Thus does Allah clarify the signs for you,) [This is] further emphasis (تَأْكِيد) and magnification (تَفْخِيم) of the rulings concluded by it, لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (so that you may apply reason.) [Meaning] the good (الخَيْر) in matters.

(٦٢) إِنَّمَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ The perfect ones (الكاملون) in faith (الإِيمان) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ from the core of their hearts وَإِذا كانُوا مَعَهُ عَلى أَمْرٍ جامِعٍ such as the Friday prayer (الجمعة), the festivals (الأعياد), wars (الحروب), and consultation (المشاورة) regarding affairs (الأمور) لَمْ يَذْهَبُوا حَتَّى يَسْتَأْذِنُوهُ they ask the permission of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He grants them permission. (62) Indeed, the faithful are only those who have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and when they are with Him for a collective matter, they do not leave until they ask His permission.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): It was revealed concerning a group of people who, when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم gathered them for an affair (لِأمْر) among the affairs, or for an expedition (بعث) He was sending, or for a war (حرب) that had commenced, would disperse without His permission. So, Allah forbade them from that.

إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَأْذِنُونَكَ أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ He repeated it emphatically (مؤكّداً) in a more eloquent (ابلغ) style (أسلوب). For it conveys that the one who seeks permission (المستأذن) is certainly (لا محالة) a believer (مؤمن), and that the one who leaves without permission (إذن) is not so. This is to clarify that seeking permission is a manifestation (مصداقاً) of the soundness of faith (صحة الإِيمان), a distinction (مميزاً) between the sincere (المخلص) and the hypocrite (المنافق), and an exaltation (تعظيماً) of the offender (للمجرم). فَإِذَا اسْتَأْذَنُوكَ لِبَعْضِ شَأْنِهِمْ ** [meaning] what important matters (المهام) occur to them. And in this [phrase] there is also exaggeration (مبالغة) and restriction (تضييق) of the matter. فَأْذَنْ لِمَنْ شِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ ** [This is] a delegation (تفويض) of the matter to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمُ اللهَ ** after granting permission (الاذن). For seeking permission (الاستيذان), even if due to an excuse (عذر), is a shortcoming (قصور), because it is prioritizing the affair of the world (الدنيا) over the affair of the religion (الدّين). إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ for the slips (لفرطات) of the servants (العباد) رَحِيمٌ for making things easy (لتيسير). Indeed, those who ask your permission are the ones who have faith in Allah and His Apostle. So when they ask your permission for some of their affairs, grant permission to whomever of them you wish, and plead with Allah for their forgiveness. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.**

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): It was revealed concerning Hanzalah ibn Abi Ayyash (حنظلة بن أبي عيّاش). That was because he married on the night preceding the morning of the Battle of Uhud (أحد). So he sought permission from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to stay with his family. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed this verse: فَأْذَنْ لِمَنْ شِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ ** (grant permission to whomever of them you wish). So he stayed with his family, then he awoke in a state of major ritual impurity (جنب). Then he attended the fighting and was martyred (استشهد). Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I saw the angels washing Hanzalah with rainwater (ماء المزن) in silver basins (صحائف فضّة) between the heaven and the earth." Thus, he was named Ghasil al-Mala'ikah (The one washed by the angels - غسيل الملائكة).

(63) لا تَجْعَلُوا دُعاءَ الرَّسُولِ بَيْنَكُمْ كَدُعاءِ بَعْضِكُمْ بَعْضاً ** (63) Do not regard the Apostle’s calling among yourselves like your calling one another. Al-Qummi said: Do not call upon the Messenger of Allah as some of you call upon others. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: He means, do not say 'O Muhammad' (يا محمّد) nor 'O Abu al-Qasim' (يا أبا القاسم), but rather say 'O Prophet of Allah' (يا نبيّ الله) and 'O Messenger of Allah' (يا رسول الله). In al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Fatimah said: When this Ayah was revealed, I feared the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from saying to him 'O Father' (يا أبة), so I used to say 'O Messenger of Allah' (يا رسول الله). He turned away from me once, or twice, or thrice. Then He turned towards me and said: "O Fatimah, it was not revealed concerning you, nor concerning your family, nor concerning your progeny. You are from me and I am from you. It was only revealed concerning the people of harshness (الجفاء) and roughness (الغلظة) from Quraysh, the people of extravagance (البذخ) and arrogance (الكبر). Say 'O Father' (يا أبةِ), for it is more life-giving to the heart and more pleasing to the Lord (الربّ)." قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللهُ الَّذِينَ يَتَسَلَّلُونَ مِنْكُمْ ** (63) Allah certainly knows those of you who slip away They exit little by little from the congregation (الجماعة). لِواذاً ** (63) stealthily. Hiding (ملاوذة), by some of them concealing themselves behind others until they exit, or by seeking refuge (يلوذ) with someone who is permitted (to leave) and then departing with him as if he were his follower. فَلْيَحْذَرِ الَّذِينَ يُخالِفُونَ عَنْ أَمْرِهِ. (63) So let those who defy His command beware, Al-Qummi: meaning, they disobey His command (أمره). أَنْ تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ ** (63) lest a trial should befall them, A trial (محنة) in the world (الدّنيا). القمّيّ A calamity (بليّة) أَوْ يُصِيبَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (or a painful punishment should afflict them). He said: He meant killing (القتل). In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: A tyrannical ruler (سلطان جائراً) will be imposed upon them, and a painful punishment (عذاب اليم) in the Hereafter (الآخرة).

(٦٤) أَلا إِنَّ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ قَدْ يَعْلَمُ ما أَنْتُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (64) Look! Whatever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Allah. He certainly knows what state you are in. (This refers to) deviation (المخالفة) and compliance (الموافقة), hypocrisy (النفاق) and sincerity (الإخلاص). He only confirmed His knowledge with the particle (قَدْ) to emphasize the threat (الوعيد). وَيَوْمَ يُرْجَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ ** (64) And the day they are returned to Him, The hypocrites (المنافقون) will be returned to Him for recompense, or it is a shift in address (الالتفات), and all of it is intended. **فَيُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما عَمِلُوا وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (64) He will inform them about what they have done, and Allah is knowing of all things. Nothing hidden is concealed from Him.

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الصادق قال : In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Fortify your wealth (أموالكم) and your private parts (فروجكم) by reciting Surat al-Nur, and fortify your women with it. For whoever habitually recites it every day or every night, none of his household will ever commit adultery until he dies. And when he dies, seventy thousand angels will accompany his funeral procession to his grave, all of them supplicating (يدعون) and seeking forgiveness (يستغفرون) from Allah for him until he enters his grave."

وفي الكافي عنه عليه السلام قال قال رسول الله صلّى الله عليه وآله : And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Do not allow women to reside in upper chambers (الغرف), and do not teach them writing (الكتابة). Teach them spinning (المغزل) and Surat al-Nur. O Allah, grant us the provision of its recitation."
